items.title: Una salida a la encrucijada de Cuba
items.description: EE. UU. lleva mucho tiempo recurriendo a la coacción para provocar un cambio político en Cuba, pero poco se compara con su campaña actual. Dado que los cubanos están pasando por enormes dificultades, este impasse no puede prolongarse indefinidamente. La mejor opción para La Habana es llevar a cabo reformas significativas, al tiempo que deja claro a Washington los peligros que entraña una intervención.

INTERVIEW – Décès de Mamadou Djouma Bah – Me David Beavogui décrypte les aspects juridiques : « lorsqu’une personne décède après une incarcération… »
items.description: Screenshot " data-large-file="https://i0.wp.com/mediaguinee.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/IMG_6870-scaled.jpeg?fit=1024%2C581&ssl=1" /> Après le décès à Kindia de Mamadou Djouma Bah, candidat au baccalauréat poursuivi dans une affaire de fraude aux examens, le débat enfle autour de la légalité de son placement en détention et des recours prévus par la loi. Dans cet entretien accordé à notre rédaction, Me David Béavogui, avocat au Barreau de Guinée, revient sur les dispositions du Code pénal, le rôle du procureur, les procédures en cas de décès en milieu carcéral et les droits de la famille. Mediaguinee : Que prévoit la loi guinéenne en cas de fraude à un examen par un candidat, notamment au regard du décès de cet élève qui était en détention ? Me David Béavogui : En pareille circonstance, il faut d’abord avoir une pensée pour la famille endeuillée et prier pour le repos de l’âme du défunt. S’agissant de la loi, les fraudes commises lors des examens ou concours publics sont prévues et réprimées par les articles 686 et 687 du Code pénal. L’article 686 dispose que toute fraude commise lors d’un examen ou d’un concours public ayant pour objet l’entrée dans une administration publique, l’obtention d’un diplôme délivré par l’État ou d’un permis de conduire est punie d’un emprisonnement d’un à trois ans, d’une amende de 500 000 à 2 500 000 francs guinéens, ou de l’une de ces deux peines seulement. L’article 687 vise notamment les personnes qui communiquent frauduleusement les sujets d’examen, utilisent de faux documents ou se substituent à un candidat. Les peines encourues vont d’un mois à trois ans d’emprisonnement, assorties d’une amende de 500 000 à 2 500 000 francs guinéens, ou de l’une de ces deux peines seulement. Vous voulez donc dire que le placement en détention d’un candidat poursuivi pour fraude est prévu par la loi ? Dans quelles conditions ? La loi vise “quiconque”, mais il faut distinguer l’infraction de la mesure de détention. La décision de placer une personne en détention relève de la procédure pénale. Le procureur peut requérir une telle mesure lorsqu’il estime qu’elle est justifiée, mais c’est l’autorité judiciaire compétente qui statue conformément aux règles de procédure. Selon les circonstances, notamment en cas de flagrant délit ou d’ouverture d’une information judiciaire, la personne poursuivie peut être maintenue en liberté ou faire l’objet d’une mesure de détention, dans les conditions prévues par la loi. La détention provisoire est-elle une mesure proportionnée pour ce type d’infraction ? Il faut distinguer la détention provisoire de la peine prononcée après jugement. La détention provisoire est une mesure prise avant que la personne ne soit définitivement jugée. Une fois la décision rendue, il ne s’agit plus d’une détention provisoire mais de l’exécution d’une peine, si une condamnation est prononcée. La loi prévoit des peines pouvant aller jusqu’à trois ans d’emprisonnement pour certaines formes de fraude aux examens. La possibilité d’une détention dépend toutefois des circonstances du dossier et des conditions fixées par le Code de procédure pénale. Concernant Mamadou Djouma Bah, drépanocytaire, qui était détenu malgré son état de santé et qui serait décédé après une hospitalisation, la loi ne pouvait-elle pas alléger sa situation ? Il existe aujourd’hui des versions contradictoires sur les circonstances de son décès. Certains affirment qu’il avait été confié à ses parents pour recevoir des soins intensifs et qu’il est décédé auprès de son médecin traitant. D’autres soutiennent qu’il est décédé alors qu’il était encore détenu. Dans ces conditions, il est difficile de se prononcer avec certitude. Toujours est-il qu’il avait fait l’objet d’une décision de justice. Les voies de recours prévues par la loi, notamment l’appel, pouvaient être exercées. Lorsqu’une personne décède après une incarcération, une autopsie peut être ordonnée afin de déterminer les causes du décès. Le rapport médico-légal est ensuite transmis au procureur de la République, qui apprécie les suites à donner. S’il estime que les circonstances du décès sont suspectes et que les éléments recueillis le justifient, il peut requérir l’ouverture d’une information judiciaire afin que les responsabilités soient établies. Quels recours la famille peut-elle exercer si elle estime que les droits du défunt n’ont pas été respectés ? En principe, la famille peut attendre la décision du ministère public. Si le procureur classe le dossier sans suite, les proches disposent notamment de la possibilité de déposer une plainte avec constitution de partie civile devant le juge d’instruction, conformément aux dispositions de la loi. Ils peuvent également exercer les autres recours ouverts par la procédure pénale afin que les responsabilités éventuelles soient recherchées et que réparation soit obtenue, le cas échéant. » Plusieurs élèves et enseignants sont actuellement détenus dans des affaires de fraude aux examens. Une partie de l’opinion demande leur libération. Est-ce juridiquement possible ? L’État de droit repose sur le respect des décisions de justice et des voies de recours prévues par la loi. Lorsqu’une condamnation est prononcée, la remise en liberté ne peut intervenir que dans les conditions prévues par les textes. Les recours, notamment l’appel lorsqu’il est recevable, permettent à une juridiction supérieure de réexaminer le dossier. Il faut laisser la procédure suivre son cours. Par ailleurs, les fraudes aux examens ne doivent pas être banalisées. Elles portent atteinte au mérite, favorisent la tricherie et fragilisent le système éducatif. Il est donc important que les infractions établies soient sanctionnées dans le respect de la loi et des droits de la défense. Réalisée par Christine Finda KAMANO L’article INTERVIEW – Décès de Mamadou Djouma Bah – Me David Beavogui décrypte les aspects juridiques : « lorsqu’une personne décède après une incarcération… » est apparu en premier sur Mediaguinee.com .

items.title: 2027: Ex-Lagos guber candidate Salis meets Atiku, plans talks with Tinubu, others
items.description: By Olasunkanmi Akoni Former Alliance for Democracy (AD) governorship candidate in Lagos State, Owolabi Salis, has held a strategic meeting with former Vice-President Atiku Abubakar as part of efforts to contribute to Nigeria’s democratic development. Salis, a New York-based lawyer and chartered accountant, disclosed that the meeting was part of consultations with key political leaders and stakeholders on national development. He said he also plans to meet President Bola Tinubu, former President Olusegun Obasanjo, and former military leaders including Gen. Yakubu Gowon, Gen. Ibrahim Babangida and Gen. Abdulsalami Abubakar. Speaking after his recent visit to Nigeria, Salis expressed concern over the country’s slow pace of development despite its vast potential more than two decades after the return to democratic rule. He attributed some of the challenges facing the country to weak democratic institutions and political practices that, according to him, have affected ordinary citizens. Salis said poverty and limited access to healthcare had widened the gap between the rich and poor, noting that many vulnerable Nigerians struggle to access basic medical services. He disclosed that he provides monthly support for some people through food and medical assistance, while also expressing concern over infrastructure challenges in disadvantaged communities. The former governorship candidate also called for electoral reforms, saying improvements were needed to strengthen confidence in the electoral process. Reflecting on his experience as a governorship candidate in the 2019 Lagos election, Salis said there was a need to improve electoral transparency and public trust in democratic institutions. He, however, said he preferred to share his ideas and proposed solutions privately with President Tinubu and other leaders rather than discuss them publicly. “I do not want to discuss this in the media, but I hope to share ideas with President Tinubu and other leaders soon,” he said. Salis, who is also an astronaut, made history in June 2023 as the first Nigerian to travel to space aboard Blue Origin’s NS-25 mission. The post 2027: Ex-Lagos guber candidate Salis meets Atiku, plans talks with Tinubu, others appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Burkina/Urgences médicales: plus de 15 500 interventions réalisés en un an
items.description: Ouagadougou, 22 juillet 2026 – Un an après son opérationnalisation, le Service d'aide médicale urgente (SAMU) s'impose progressivement comme un acteur majeur de la prise en charge des urgences préhospitalières au Burkina Faso. En douze mois d'activité, le service a traité 216 459 appels et réalisé 15 531 interventions médicales, témoignant de son rôle croissant dans le sauvetage de vies humaines. Ces résultats ont été présentés par le directeur général du SAMU, Pr Flavien Kaboré, à l'occasion d'une visite de presse organisée ce mercredi 22 juillet 2026, marquant le premier anniversaire du service. Au total, 4 121 interventions primaires ont été réalisées directement sur les lieux de l'urgence, permettant aux équipes médicalisées de porter assistance aux victimes avant leur évacuation vers les structures de santé appropriées. Le SAMU a également assuré 11 219 transferts médicalisés entre établissements de santé afin de garantir la continuité des soins. Disponibles 24 heures sur 24 et 7 jours sur 7, les équipes du Service mobile d'urgence et de réanimation (SMUR) interviennent lors des accidents de la circulation, des malaises, des urgences obstétricales, des traumatismes et de toute autre situation de détresse vitale. Grâce à leur expertise, les premiers soins sont administrés sur place avant le transfert du patient lorsque son état le nécessite. Déployé à Ouagadougou et à Bobo-Dioulasso, le SAMU poursuit son rapprochement avec les populations à travers des équipes prépositionnées dans plusieurs formations sanitaires stratégiques. Le directeur général a rappelé que les interventions primaires sont gratuites, tout comme les transferts médicalisés des femmes enceintes et des enfants de moins de cinq ans, conformément aux mesures prises par le Gouvernement pour faciliter l'accès aux soins d'urgence. Le Pr Flavien Kaboré a toutefois attiré l'attention sur un défi majeur : 48 % des appels reçus ne concernent pas de véritables urgences médicales. Il a invité les populations à faire un usage responsable du numéro vert 15, afin de permettre aux équipes d'intervenir rapidement auprès des personnes dont la vie est réellement en danger. Au-delà des chiffres, cette première année d'activité confirme l'utilité du SAMU dans le renforcement du système de santé burkinabè. Chaque intervention réalisée traduit une volonté constante d'offrir aux populations une prise en charge rapide, médicalisée et de qualité lorsque chaque minute compte. Pour toute intervention du SAMU à Ouagadougou où à Bobo-Dioulasso, contactez le 15. DCRP/MS L’article Burkina/Urgences médicales: plus de 15 500 interventions réalisés en un an est apparu en premier sur Wakat Séra .

items.title: 2027: Credible polls require responsible media, transparent INEC, informed voters — Akhaine, Editors
items.description: …Stakeholders advocate renaming LASU after Lateef Jakande By Olasunkanmi Akoni and Juliet Umeh A professor of Political Science at Lagos State University (LASU), Sylvester Akhaine, and the Nigerian Guild of Editors (NGE) have stressed the need for a responsible media, transparent electoral commission and informed electorate to guarantee credible elections in Nigeria. They made the call at the Fourth Lateef Jakande Memorial Lecture organised by the NGE on Thursday in Ikeja, Lagos, with the theme: “The Media, INEC, Voters and the Path to Credible Elections.” The annual lecture is organised in honour of the late Alhaji Lateef Jakande, former Lagos State Governor, journalist and former President of the NGE. The event was attended by NGE members led by its President and Editor of Vanguard Media, Mr Eze Anaba; Vanguard Publisher, Uncle Sam Amuka; veteran journalist and co-founder of Newswatch, Mr Ray Ekpu; members of the Jakande family, including his widow, Alhaja Sikirat Jakande; academics, media executives, public officials, civil society representatives and students from LASU, Yaba College of Technology and other institutions. Delivering the keynote address, Akhaine said the role of the media, the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) and voters would be critical to the success of the 2027 general elections. Referencing the ideals of the Jakande administration during the Second Republic, Akhaine described the theme of the lecture as timely, given the country’s preparations for another electoral cycle. “For credible elections to take place in our country, the media, INEC and voters have a role to play,” he said. Akhaine described Nigeria’s democratic experience as facing challenges of state capture and weakened sovereignty, where institutional balance is affected and non-state actors exert influence in some areas. He said such challenges had contributed to insecurity and increased reliance on community-based security arrangements alongside conventional security agencies. The political scientist also criticised Nigeria’s political culture, citing monetised politics, poor leadership recruitment and a trust deficit that has reduced citizens’ active participation in governance. He described the media as a key institution for information dissemination, public education and shaping opinion, noting its historical role in Nigeria’s democratic development. On electoral management, Akhaine traced the evolution of Nigeria’s election bodies from the Electoral Commission of Nigeria (ECN) established in 1959 to the present INEC, established in 1998. He said while election management bodies expanded globally following the third wave of democratisation, many in Africa still face challenges relating to independence. Akhaine described voters as the “transmission belt for consent” in democracy, stressing that citizens remain central to any electoral process. According to him, credible elections are best identified by the absence of practices such as ballot stuffing, vote-buying, gerrymandering and intimidation. He referenced international frameworks, including the International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights (ICCPR), the African Charter on Human and Peoples’ Rights, the ECOWAS Protocol and SADC Guidelines, which promote free, fair and transparent elections. Reviewing Nigeria’s electoral history, Akhaine said none of the seven general elections conducted since 1999 could be regarded as completely free and fair. He cited previous electoral controversies, including the 1964/1965 elections, the 1979 “two-thirds” controversy, the annulment of the June 12, 1993 presidential election and the 2007 elections, which the Commonwealth Observer Group said fell short of acceptable standards. He also identified emerging challenges facing the media, including misinformation, fake news and post-truth politics driven by digital platforms, bots and online manipulation. Call for collaboration Akhaine urged the media, INEC and voters to work together to strengthen Nigeria’s democracy. He said the media must uphold the provisions of Sections 22 and 39 of the 1999 Constitution, which empower the press to hold government accountable and guarantee freedom of expression. He urged INEC to maximise technological reforms and financial autonomy to improve electoral credibility. Akhaine also supported recommendations of the Uwais Electoral Reform Panel, including the proposal to remove the appointment of INEC officials from the exclusive control of the executive arm. He called for continuous voter education to help citizens make informed choices and resist electoral inducements. “If these critical stakeholders play their role, it is my conviction that Nigeria’s democratic train would be steered on the right course,” he said. Earlier, NGE President Eze Anaba said the choice of theme was informed by the country’s approach to another electoral cycle and the need to strengthen democratic institutions. He said the credibility of elections remained the foundation of legitimate governance, warning that doubts over electoral integrity could erode public confidence in democratic institutions. Anaba urged the media to remain professional, accurate and fair in election coverage, while calling on INEC to continue to discharge its responsibilities with transparency, competence and impartiality. He explained that the memorial lecture, instituted in 2023, was designed to preserve the legacy of Jakande, whose career as a journalist, administrator and public servant represented service, integrity and good governance. The Chairman of the occasion, Prof. Lai Olorode, described Jakande as a leader committed to public welfare and urged political leaders to emulate his values. He said credible elections remained essential to ensuring that the will of the people is reflected in governance. Lagos State Governor Babajide Sanwo-Olu, represented by the Commissioner for Information and Strategy, Mr Gbenga Omotoso, described Jakande as an outstanding journalist and administrator whose contributions continue to inspire generations. Sanwo-Olu warned against the spread of fake news and misinformation, urging media practitioners to uphold accuracy, credibility and ethical standards. He also welcomed efforts by the Federal Government to address challenges facing media organisations, particularly the relationship between traditional media and global digital platforms. Meanwhile, stakeholders at the event advocated the renaming of Lagos State University (LASU) after Lateef Jakande, describing the proposal as a fitting recognition of his contributions to education, journalism and public service. The post 2027: Credible polls require responsible media, transparent INEC, informed voters — Akhaine, Editors appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Video: ‘Don’t let me die’ – ailing Nollywood actress Ngozi Nwosu appeals for help
items.description: Veteran Nollywood actress Ngozi Nwosu has made an emotional appeal for financial assistance as she battles an undisclosed illness, revealing that she has been hospitalised for three weeks and is preparing to undergo three surgeries. The actress shared the heartfelt video on her Instagram page on Thursday, where she appeared from her hospital bed, visibly emotional as she appealed to fans, friends and family members not to abandon her during the difficult period. View this post on Instagram A post shared by Ngozi Nwosu (@officialngozinwosu) Addressing her supporters, Nwosu explained why she had been absent from public view in recent weeks. “My people, my fans and my family, I know that you’ve not seen me in circulation for some time. I’ve been hospitalized now for three weeks. Please, the thing has gone beyond my control. I need your help, don’t let me die.” The veteran actress said she remained hopeful that she would overcome the health challenge but pleaded for support as she continued to endure severe pain. “I know that you people never forsake me and I know that God will always see me through, but I’m begging you, my friends and family, don’t let me die because I am going through a lot as in pain.” Nwosu also urged anyone willing to assist to contact her management team, concluding her appeal with a prayer. “My sickness will never be anybody’s portion. In Jesus’ name, thank you.” Although the actress did not disclose the nature of her illness, she revealed in an earlier appeal that she requires three surgeries, with the procedures expected to cost about ₦30 million. Vanguard News The post Video: ‘Don’t let me die’ – ailing Nollywood actress Ngozi Nwosu appeals for help appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Fasofoot : La FBF mise sur la formation de base des arbitres de Ligue 3 pour l’avenir du football burkinabè
items.description: La Fédération burkinabè de football (FBF) poursuit son vaste programme de formation des acteurs du ballon rond. Après les sessions destinées aux arbitres de Ligue 1 et Ligue 2, c’est au tour de ceux de Ligue 3 de bénéficier d’un stage intensif de 72 heures, mêlant théorie et pratique. Le Presidium. Le président de la FBF en pagne tissé deuxième à partir de la gauche Pour le président de la FBF, Oumarou Sawadogo, cet effort de formation des acteurs du football est un pilier de son mandat. « La formation est un des piliers de mon thème de campagne. Presque tous les acteurs seront formés, car des gens bien formés, cela limite les erreurs dans l’administration du football », a-t-il fait savoir. Le président Oumarou Sawadogo a insisté sur l’importance cruciale de cette catégorie d’arbitres. « Vous êtes à la base de votre formation. Quand la base est ratée, tout le reste ne sert à rien. Vous arbitrez sur des terrains sans sécurité, vous êtes les plus exposés. Essayez d’être les meilleurs possibles », les a-t-il adressés. Le président Sawadogo a tenu un discours sans détour, appelant à la rigueur et au dépassement de soi. « Après un match, remettez-vous en question. Même au niveau mondial, il n’y a pas d’arbitre sans formation. L’arbitrage vient en appoint de ce que vous faites », mes a-t-il prodigués comme conseil. Oumarou Sawadogo a aussi mis en garde les arbitres contre les dérives. « Vous prenez les matchs en otage… Cessez vos problèmes de personnes. Notre football est miné par de faux problèmes », a-t-il prévenu. Enfin, M. Sawadogo a fixé un objectif ambitieux pour le football burkinabè. « Nous avons postulé pour la CAN 2032. Si parmi nos trois pays, il n’y a aucun arbitre burkinabè, cela voudra dire que nous avons échoué », a-t-il alerté. Des arbitres de Ligue 3 en formation Sur le contenu de la formation, l’instructeur technique Drissa Mondès Sessouma a détaillé le programme riche. « Nous avons commencé par un check physique, car le début de la saison approche. En salle, nous aborderons les duels, le hors-jeu, la faute de main, la gestion du match, ainsi que le positionnement et le déplacement sur le terrain », a-t-il expliqué. L'instructeur technique a précisé que ces phases théoriques seront complétées par des exercices pratiques quotidiens sur le terrain. Cette session de 72 heures s’inscrit dans une dynamique de formation continue, avec un minimum de dix sessions annuelles recommandées par la FIFA, souvent dépassé par la FBF. L’objectif est de préparer la relève et hisser les arbitres burkinabè au niveau des compétitions internationales. Lire aussi : Football : La FBF renforce les capacités de 50 jeunes arbitres à Ouagadougou Minute.bf

items.title: Full audit of payables conducted to eliminate irregular obligations – Ato Forson
items.description: Finance Minister Dr. Cassiel Ato Forson says a comprehensive audit of government payables has been conducted to identify and eliminate irregular obligations as part of measures to strengthen public financial management. Presenting the 2026 Mid-Year Budget Review in Parliament, Dr Forson said the audit was aimed at identifying and eliminating irregular commitments that had contributed to fiscal pressures in previous years. “We undertook a comprehensive audit of government payables to eliminate irregular obligations,” he told Parliament. According to the Finance Minister, the exercise forms part of broader reforms introduced by government to restore expenditure discipline, improve accountability and ensure that public resources are used efficiently. Dr Forson said government has also strengthened procurement controls by linking spending commitments directly to approved budgets. “We also enforced expenditure control. Government amended the Public Procurement Act to require commitment authorisation before procurement.” He explained that the reform ensures that procurement decisions are backed by available budgetary allocations, preventing government institutions from entering into commitments that later become financial burdens on the state. READ ALSO: Ato Forson says government will not seek supplementary estimates in 2026 Mid-Year Budget Review “This reform firmly linked procurement to approved budget and restored discipline to public expenditure management across the government.” The Finance Minister added that the measures are part of efforts to reduce fiscal risks and prevent the recurrence of unpaid obligations that have historically increased government liabilities. He said the reforms, together with stronger oversight institutions, would help improve transparency, accountability and value for money in public spending.

items.title: Nayala: un bureau provincial de la jeunesse désigné par consensus en attendant sa nomination officielle
items.description: Burkina-Nayala-désignation-Bureau-Conseil-Provincial-Jeunesse Nayala: un bureau provincial de la jeunesse désigné par consensus en attendant sa nomination officielle Toma, le 21 juil. 2026 (AIB) – Les représentants des organisations de jeunesse de la province du Nayala ont désigné, mardi à Toma, les membres du bureau exécutif provincial du Conseil national consultatif de la jeunesse. La rencontre, présidée par le Haut-commissaire de la province, Honoré Frédéric Paré, marque une étape dans la relance des instances de représentation des jeunes. Le Haut-commissaire a rappelé que les jeunes étaient restés sans véritable représentation à la suite de la suspension des anciennes instances. Il a indiqué que l'adoption de nouveaux textes en 2026 a permis de relancer le processus de mise en place des organes de jeunesse dans les provinces. Il a également expliqué la composition du bureau exécutif provincial, les conditions d'éligibilité ainsi que les critères relatifs à l'envergure provinciale des associations, à la moralité des candidats et à leur conformité avec la nouvelle législation sur les associations. À l'issue des échanges, les neuf responsables ont été désignés par consensus et par acclamation. Il s'agit de Guiguemdé Ardjouma (président), Djibo Soumaïla (vice-président), Toé Siaka (secrétaire général), Ouédraogo Hamidou (trésorier général), Ky Lydie (trésorière générale adjointe), Diébé Nathalie et Toé Théophile (rapporteurs généraux), Zéyé Abdoul Aziz (genre et promotion des jeunes) et Ki Souleymane (communication et partenariat). Le Haut-commissaire a précisé que ces responsables entreront officiellement en fonction après leur nomination par arrêté de la ministre chargée de la Jeunesse. Il les a invités à exercer leur mission dans un esprit de volontariat, de responsabilité et de service au profit de la jeunesse et du développement de la province du Nayala. Agence d'Information du Burkina KTA/bak

items.title: Gov’t has spent GH¢48.8bn on public sector wages, GH¢21.5bn on interest payments – Ato Forson
items.description: Finance Minister Dr Cassiel Ato Forson has outlined government’s expenditure commitments in the first half of 2026, rejecting claims that the administration has slowed down spending on approved programmes. Presenting the 2026 Mid-Year Budget Review in Parliament on Thursday, July 24, Dr Forson said the government had continued to deploy public resources in a responsible manner, guided by the principle of spending within available means while prioritising national development needs. He said the government had so far paid GH¢48.8 billion for the compensation of public sector workers, including GH¢4 billion in contributions to the Social Security and National Insurance Trust (SSNIT) and the Tier 2 Pension Scheme. “Rt. Hon. Speaker, in recent times, a narrative has emerged suggesting that this government is not spending enough. Nothing could be farther from the truth,” Dr Forson told Parliament. He added that government had also fulfilled its domestic and external debt obligations, paying GH¢21.5 billion in interest payments and US$700 million towards Eurobond debt service and interest obligations. The Finance Minister said the expenditure demonstrates the government’s commitment to meeting its obligations while maintaining transparency, accountability and fiscal discipline in the management of public finances.

items.title: Près de 4 tonnes de cocaïne estimée à 317 millions de dollars interceptés
items.description: Les autorités libériennes ont annoncé avoir saisi, le mardi 21 juillet 2026, près de quatre tonnes de cocaïne, d'une valeur estimée à plus de 317 millions de dollars. Présentée comme la plus importante saisie de drogue jamais réalisée dans le pays, cette opération a conduit à l'arrestation de plusieurs suspects, dont deux ressortissants étrangers, et met en lumière l'ampleur d'un réseau de trafic actif depuis plusieurs années. Le Liberia a réalisé une saisie historique dans sa lutte contre le trafic de stupéfiants. Selon un communiqué du ministère de la Justice, relayé par plusieurs médias, les forces de l'ordre ont mis la main, le 21 juillet 2026, sur environ 3 971 kilogrammes de cocaïne, dont la valeur est estimée à 317,68 millions de dollars sur le marché noir. Les autorités précisent que cette opération constitue la plus importante saisie de drogue jamais enregistrée au Liberia. Cette intervention est l'aboutissement d'une enquête ouverte en juin 2026, après la découverte de 230 kilogrammes de cocaïne à l'aéroport international Roberts. Les investigations menées à la suite de cette première saisie ont permis aux enquêteurs de remonter la filière et de localiser une importante cargaison dans le comté de Duazon, le long de la route menant à l'aéroport. Les investigations ont révélé l'existence d'«un réseau criminel structuré», actif au Liberia depuis plus de six ans. Selon les autorités, les trafiquants utilisaient un système de fret organisé à l'aéroport Roberts, en dissimulant la drogue dans des cargaisons déclarées comme des marchandises ordinaires, notamment des épices ou des produits textiles, à l'aide de documents falsifiés. L'opération a également conduit à l'interpellation de plusieurs personnes, parmi lesquelles un ressortissant serbe et un individu possédant les nationalités colombienne et espagnole. Les autorités poursuivent leurs investigations afin d'identifier l'ensemble des membres du réseau. La lutte contre le trafic de drogue demeure un enjeu majeur pour le gouvernement libérien. En juin dernier, la fuite de quatre suspects avant leur arrestation avait suscité de vives critiques de plusieurs sénateurs, qui avaient dénoncé les failles du dispositif sécuritaire et le rôle grandissant du Liberia comme plateforme de transit entre les pays producteurs d'Amérique latine et les marchés européens. Cette saisie record illustre la volonté des autorités libériennes de renforcer leur offensive contre les réseaux internationaux de trafic de drogue. Les investigations se poursuivent afin de démanteler entièrement cette organisation et de consolider les dispositifs de contrôle face à un phénomène qui place le Liberia au cœur des routes du narcotrafic international. Par Valentin SOMANDE L’article Près de 4 tonnes de cocaïne estimée à 317 millions de dollars interceptés est apparu en premier sur Wakat Séra .

items.title: Gov’t to keep spending within approved limits while cutting VAT burdens – Ato Forson
items.description: Finance Minister Dr Cassiel Ato Forson has reaffirmed the government’s commitment to fiscal discipline, assuring Parliament that expenditure will remain within approved limits while efforts continue to improve the efficiency and effectiveness of public spending. Speaking during the presentation of the 2026 Mid-Year Fiscal Policy Review in Parliament on Thursday, Dr Forson said prudent expenditure management remains central to the government’s fiscal strategy. “On the expenditure side, government remains firmly committed to keeping spending within approved limits while improving its efficiency and effectiveness,” he told Parliament. The Finance Minister also outlined what he described as the most ambitious reforms to Ghana’s Value Added Tax (VAT) system in more than a decade, saying the measures are aimed at eliminating longstanding distortions, improving efficiency and supporting business growth. “Mr Speaker, this government introduced the most ambitious reforms to Ghana’s VAT system in over a decade to eliminate long-standing distortions, improve efficiency and support business growth,” he said. According to Dr Forson, the government has abolished the COVID-19 Health Recovery Levy and restructured the VAT system by decoupling the Ghana Education Trust Fund (GETFund) Levy and the National Health Insurance Levy (NHIL) from the VAT base, making both eligible for input tax deductions. He added that the reforms have reduced the effective VAT rate from 21.9 per cent to 20 per cent, abolished VAT on the exploration and prospecting for minerals, thereby relieving thousands of micro and small businesses of the requirement to register for and charge VAT, and extended the zero-rating of locally manufactured sanitary pads until 2028. “We abolished the COVID-19 Health Recovery Levy. We decoupled the GETFund Levy and the National Health Insurance Levy from the VAT base, making both eligible for input tax deduction. We reduced the effective VAT rate from 21.9% to 20%. We abolished VAT on exploration and prospecting for minerals, relieving thousands of micro and small businesses of the obligation to register for and charge VAT. And we extended the zero-rating of locally manufactured sanitary pads to 2028,” he said. Dr Forson said the reforms are expected to simplify tax administration, reduce the burden on businesses and improve tax compliance. “These reforms have simplified the VAT system, removed distortions, lowered the tax burden and strengthened the foundation for improved compliance,” he stated.

items.title: Telcos seek stronger infrastructure protection to bridge Nigeria’s digital divide
items.description: By Juliet Umeh & Islamiyyah Adekanbi The Association of Licensed Telecommunications Operators of Nigeria (ALTON) has called for stronger protection of telecommunications infrastructure, saying sustained investment, innovation and collaboration are essential to closing Nigeria’s digital divide. Chairman of ALTON, Gbenga Adebayo, made the call at the Nigeria Information Technology Reporters Association (NITRA) Innovative and Scientific Conference in Lagos. Adebayo said the telecommunications industry had transformed Nigeria’s economy over the past 25 years, growing from fewer than 400,000 connected telephone lines before the GSM era to more than 170 million active subscriptions. He described the sector as one of Africa’s major success stories in economic and digital transformation, noting that telecommunications now supports banking, education, healthcare, commerce, governance, security and entertainment. According to him, every digital payment, online learning platform, telemedicine service and government digital platform relies on telecommunications infrastructure. Adebayo said operators had invested billions of dollars in mobile networks, fibre infrastructure, data centres, cloud platforms and 5G deployment, but noted that challenges including multiple taxation, inconsistent Right-of-Way policies, vandalism, high energy costs and regulatory bottlenecks continued to affect growth. He said the focus of the industry had moved beyond expanding coverage to ensuring reliable, resilient and high-quality connectivity for Nigerians. “Quality of service cannot be improved through fines and penalties alone. Network performance depends on several factors beyond operators’ control, including electricity supply, fibre cuts, vandalism, Right-of-Way challenges and insecurity,” he said. The ALTON chairman described telecommunications infrastructure as the backbone of Nigeria’s digital economy and urged citizens to protect critical assets. He commended the Federal Government for designating telecom facilities as Critical National Information Infrastructure (CNII), and praised the Nigerian Communications Commission (NCC) for what he described as a consultative approach to regulation. On the ongoing review of Mobile Termination Rates (MTR), Adebayo said no decision had been reached, adding that the NCC was conducting an evidence-based assessment to ensure future decisions reflect economic realities, encourage competition and sustain investment in the sector. He reaffirmed ALTON’s commitment to collaborating with government, regulators, NITRA and other stakeholders to build a telecommunications sector that is resilient, innovative, inclusive and globally competitive. The post Telcos seek stronger infrastructure protection to bridge Nigeria’s digital divide appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: ‘Your money is safe’ — Ato Forson pledges transparency, fiscal discipline in managing Ghana’s economy
items.description: Finance Minister Dr Cassiel Ato Forson has assured Ghanaians that their money is secure under the stewardship of the current administration, pledging to manage public finances with honesty, prudence and unwavering accountability while promising never to misrepresent the country’s economic situation. Delivering the closing remarks of the 2026 Mid-Year Budget Review in Parliament on Thursday, July 23, Dr Forson said the ultimate responsibility of a Finance Minister is not measured by the volume of government expenditure but by how effectively public resources are safeguarded for the benefit of citizens. “The true measure of a Finance Minister is not how much he spends, but how faithfully he protects the resources entrusted to him,” he told Parliament. The Finance Minister stressed that every resource managed by the government belongs to the Ghanaian people, making transparency and responsible stewardship a moral obligation rather than simply a constitutional duty. “The money we manage does not belong to those entrusted to manage it. It belongs to the people of Ghana – it belongs to you!” he declared. Dr Forson noted that every tax payment, every loan secured and every government expenditure must be guided by the national interest and the need to ensure value for money. “Every cedi collected in taxes, every cedi borrowed and every cedi spent carries with it a sacred obligation to use it wisely, honestly and always in the national interest,” he said. Seeking to reassure Parliament, investors and the wider public about the state of Ghana’s finances, Dr Forson offered what he described as a simple but important assurance. “My assurance to this House and to every Ghanaian is simple: your money is safe.” He added that under President John Dramani Mahama’s administration, the government remains committed to maintaining sound economic management and protecting the country’s financial stability. “Under the leadership of President Mahama, your economy is in safe hands. And as long as I have the honour to serve as Minister for Finance, it will remain so.” Dr Forson also pledged that the government would remain open and truthful with Ghanaians about the country’s public finances, regardless of whether the economic outlook was favourable or challenging. “I pledge to level with you always. We will tell you the truth about the state of our public finances, whether the news is welcome or difficult.” He assured Parliament that the government would neither downplay existing economic challenges nor overstate its achievements for political convenience. “We will neither conceal our challenges nor exaggerate our successes.” According to the Finance Minister, transparency is essential to maintaining public trust and ensuring sound economic governance. “Trust is built on truth, and truth must remain the foundation of sound economic management.” While acknowledging improvements in the country’s economic performance, Dr Forson emphasised that government’s responsibility extends beyond achieving short-term success. Instead, he said, the focus must be on preserving the progress made and ensuring that future generations inherit a stable and resilient economy. “Our responsibility now is not merely to celebrate these gains but to preserve them for future generations.” He stressed that economic stability requires consistent discipline and responsible leadership rather than isolated policy interventions. “Economic stability is not an event. It is a duty renewed every day through prudent stewardship, fiscal discipline and unwavering accountability.” Ending his address on an optimistic note, Dr Forson declared that Ghana’s economic recovery was firmly on course and that the country would continue advancing under the current administration. He credited both President Mahama’s leadership and the resilience of Ghanaians for the country’s progress. “Under the leadership of President Mahama, and with the determination of the Ghanaian people, Ghana is moving forward, steadily, honestly and together,” he concluded.

items.title: Karachi police claim suspect in six-year-old girl's abduction, murder killed in shootout
items.description: KARACHI: The police on Thursday killed the alleged abductor and murderer of a six-year-old girl in a shootout after the suspect allegedly opened fire on law enforcers during a raid in Karachi’s Keamari area. According to a statement issued by the Keamari police spokesperson, a first information report (No. 265/2026) under Sections 364-A and 302 of the Pakistan Penal Code was registered at Jackson Police Station regarding the abduction and murder of a six-year-old girl. The spokesperson stated that police obtained CCTV footage from various locations along with other available evidence, which was thoroughly examined during the investigation. “Based on the CCTV footage and information gathered during the investigation, police received credible information regarding the whereabouts of a suspect,” the spokesperson said. Acting on this information, he said, a police party reached the Mubarak Mosque area, within the jurisdiction of Jackson Police Station. “However, upon seeing the police party, the suspect opened fire on the officers, prompting the police to retaliate,” he claimed. “During the exchange of gunfire, the individual sustained gunshot injuries and later died,” the spokesperson further claimed. Immediately after the incident, he added that the crime scene was secured and all relevant evidence was taken into custody in accordance with legal procedures. “A separate case regarding the police encounter is being registered at Jackson Police Station, while legal and forensic proceedings related to the incident are also underway.” The police spokesperson added that an investigation into the abduction and murder of the young girl was “continuing comprehensively in light of CCTV footage, medico-legal findings, forensic evidence, technical analysis and other available evidence”. Meanwhile, the body of the six-year-old girl was recovered from bushes in the Manora area, police said. According to Police Surgeon Dr Sumaiyya Syed, “The victim was subjected to sexual violence.” “There are multiple marks of violence all over the body,” she added. “All samples have been collected. The cause of death has been reserved pending the reports.” Earlier this month, police arrested a 20-year-old man for the kidnapping, rape and murder of a six-year-old boy before allegedly dumping the victim’s body, wrapped in a gunny bag, onto an empty plot from the third floor.

items.title: Gov’t to scrap 20% excise duty on locally produced fruit juice to boost agro-processing
items.description: The government has announced plans to abolish the 20 per cent excise duty on locally manufactured fruit juices as part of a broader package of tax reforms aimed at supporting agro-processing, creating jobs and strengthening domestic industry. Presenting the 2026 Mid-Year Budget Review in Parliament on Thursday, July 23, the Finance Minister, Dr Cassiel Ato Forson, said the measure forms part of reforms under the proposed Excise Duty Bill, which seeks to improve tax administration, close revenue leakages and promote local production. Among the key reforms, Dr Forson said the government would “abolish the payment of 20 percent excise duty on locally manufactured fruit juices introduced in 2023.” He explained that the decision is intended “to support agro-processing and job creation.” The announcement comes as the government seeks to encourage value addition in the agricultural sector by reducing the tax burden on local manufacturers and making locally processed products more competitive. Beyond the fruit juice tax relief, the Finance Minister said the government had identified significant weaknesses in the country’s excise tax system, particularly in the taxation of wines and spirits. He disclosed that an analysis of imports between 2023 and 2025 showed that wines and spirits with a taxable value of more than GH¢5 billion entered the country, but about 78 per cent of that value passed through customs procedures such as warehousing, transit, temporary admission and free zones without attracting excise duty. “In other words, almost four out of every five cedis of the potential excise tax base on wine and spirits escaped the tax net,” he said. “Only one cedi out of every five was subject to excise duty. That is neither efficient nor sustainable, and it must change.” To address the problem, the Minister said the Excise Duty Bill proposes a hybrid tax system that combines value-based and quantity-based taxation for wines and spirits to reduce undervaluation and misclassification.

items.title: Gov’t honours Eurobond obligations with US$700m payment – Finance Minister
items.description: Finance Minister Dr Cassiel Ato Forson says the government has honoured its international debt obligations by paying US$700 million towards Eurobond debt service and interest payments. Speaking during the 2026 Mid-Year Budget Review in Parliament on Thursday, July 24, Dr Forson said the payment forms part of the government’s broader commitment to restoring confidence in Ghana’s economy and maintaining credibility with external creditors. He said government remains focused on meeting its financial obligations while pursuing measures to protect the country’s fiscal stability. “In the spirit of transparency and accountability, allow me to lay before this House a clear account of some of the expenditure we have made so far in support of Government programmes and sectors approved in the 2026 Budget,” he said. Dr Forson said the payments demonstrate government’s commitment to responsible financial management and ensuring that the country continues to meet its obligations despite economic challenges.

items.title: Última hora de los incendios forestales, en directo | El incendio en Ávila obliga a confinar Burgohondo, El Tiemblo y Navaluenga
items.description: El incendio declarado este jueves en Ávila avanza sin control y ha obligado a confinar los municipios de Burgohondo, El Tiemblo y Navaluenga. El resto de fuegos en Castilla y León evolucionan favorablemente. El trabajo del operativo de extinción ha permitido rebajar la gravedad en los fuegos de Rábano de Aliste (Zamora) y Retortillo (Soria), ya sin confinamientos ni desalojados. El de Castropodame (León) evoluciona favorablemente, pero continúan desalojadas las localidades de Turienzo Castañero y San Pedro Castañero. En Madrid, la presidenta de la Comunidad, Isabel Díaz Ayuso, ha advertido de que las próximas horas del incendio forestal en Villa del Prado, originado en la localidad toledana de Almorox y que ya ha calcinado 890 hectáreas, serán “muy complicadas” debido a las altas temperaturas y el viento. El fuego está ya perimetrado en la zona de Almorox y ha bajado al nivel 1 de gravedad. La vicepresidenta tercera del Gobierno y ministra para la Transición Ecológica, Sara Aagesen, ha puesto cifras a los incendios que asolan España: 108.000 hectáreas quemadas en lo que llevamos de año, cuatro veces más de lo que había ardido el año pasado en estas fechas. Seguir leyendo

items.title: Le ministère de l’hydraulique : « la station de décantation de Beni Naaji a su maintenir la production de l’eau du projet Aftout malgré la hausse du niveau de turbidité »
items.description: Le ministère mauritanien de l'hydraulique et de l’assainissement a déclaré que l'approvisionnement en eau de la ville de Nouakchott via le réseau côtier d'Afout se poursuivait de manière normale et stable, malgré la hausse enregistrée du niveau de turbidité des eaux du fleuve Sénégal pour la troisième journée consécutive en raison des conditions saisonnières. Le ministère a ajouté que les données techniques indiquaient que le niveau de turbidité dans les eaux du fleuve avait atteint 2 600 NTU, précisant que la station de décantation de Beni Naaji avait continué à fonctionner à une efficacité opérationnelle de 100 %, ce qui a permis de maintenir un débit de production de 148 800 mètres cubes par jour. Le maintien de la production à ce niveau pendant la saison des pluies actuelle fait suite à l’inauguration, l’année dernière, de l’installation de décantation de Beni Naaji. C’est la première fois que cette installation est confrontée à une élévation des niveaux de sédiments dans les eaux du fleuve à cette période de l’année, alors qu’auparavant ce phénomène affectait la production d’eau et provoquait des perturbations dans l’approvisionnement de la ville de Nouakchott. Le ministère a indiqué que les différentes installations de production du réseau d’Aftout-Sahli fonctionnent normalement et que la hausse du niveau de sédiments n’a pas affecté la continuité de la production d’eau, grâce à l’efficacité de l’installation de décantation.

Les habitants esperent desormais un approvisionnement en carburant pour energie du Mali(EDM) afin de mettre fin aux coupures d'electricite qui durent depuis plus d'un mois. Just In: Enugu Air aircraft overshoots runway in Benin airport; ‘Everyone’s safe’
items.description: By Dickson Omobola, Benin City Enugu State-owned carrier, Enugu Air, has confirmed that one of its aircraft suffered a runway excursion after landing at the Benin Airport today. Read Also: El-Rufai’s family disowns purported letter to ADC leadership The airline disclosed the incident in a statement posted on its official X account, saying all passengers and crew members on board were safely evacuated without injuries. The statement reads: “Enugu Air wishes to inform the public that one of our aircraft operating into Benin experienced a runway excursion after landing today. “We confirm that all passengers and crew have safely disembarked and there were no injuries or casualties. “The aircraft has been secured and the relevant aviation authorities have been notified in line with established procedures. An assessment of the aircraft and the circumstances surrounding the occurrence is currently underway. “As a result, there may be temporary adjustments to some flight schedules. Passengers affected by any changes will be contacted directly and provided with the necessary assistance. The safety of our passengers, crew, and operations remains our highest priority.” The post Just In: Enugu Air aircraft overshoots runway in Benin airport; ‘Everyone’s safe’ appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: IVes championnats d’athlétisme U17 et U20 des cinq nations : Le Burkina entend confirmer son leadership à domicile
items.description: La IV e édition des championnats d’athlétisme U17 et U20 des cinq nations s’annonce particulièrement relevée, mais les Burkinabè se sont préparés en conséquence afin de réaffirmer leur domination sur la compétition. L’information ainsi que d’autres détails de l’événement ont été portés à la connaissance des journalistes par les organisateurs lors d’une conférence de presse le jeudi 23 juillet 2026 à Ouagadougou. Ils seront environ 200 athlètes et encadreurs du Bénin, de la Côte d’Ivoire, du Ghana, du Togo et du Burkina Faso à se mesurer à la faveur des IV es championnats d’athlétisme U17 et U20 des cinq nations. Prévus pour se tenir dans la capitale burkinabè les 25 et 26 juillet 2026, l’événement va concerner 32 épreuves au total soit 16 épreuves masculines et 16 épreuves féminines, a fait savoir le 1 er vice-président de la fédération burkinabè d’athlétisme Missiri Sawadogo. Toutes les épreuves en athlétisme sont prises en compte à l’exception du lancer de marteau et de la perche. Cette compétition se tient à une période charnière de l’année en ce sens qu’elle constitue un tremplin pour la préparation des athlètes aux échéances internationales majeures des catégories concernées, a souligné les conférenciers. Il s’agit notamment des championnats du monde juniors des U20 à Eugène aux USA prévus pour le mois d’août et les jeux olympiques de la jeunesse à Dakar en octobre-novembre. Les journalistes ont été rassurés de la présence effective des délégations des cinq pays. " data-large-file="https://www.sidwaya.info/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/02-1024x461.jpg" class="size-full wp-image-166208" src="https://www.sidwaya.info/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/02-scaled.jpg" alt="" width="2560" height="1152" srcset="https://www.sidwaya.info/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/02-scaled.jpg 2560w, https://www.sidwaya.info/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/02-300x135.jpg 300w, https://www.sidwaya.info/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/02-1024x461.jpg 1024w, https://www.sidwaya.info/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/02-768x346.jpg 768w, https://www.sidwaya.info/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/02-1536x691.jpg 1536w, https://www.sidwaya.info/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/02-2048x922.jpg 2048w, https://www.sidwaya.info/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/02-933x420.jpg 933w, https://www.sidwaya.info/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/02-150x68.jpg 150w, https://www.sidwaya.info/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/02-696x313.jpg 696w, https://www.sidwaya.info/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/02-1068x481.jpg 1068w, https://www.sidwaya.info/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/02-1920x864.jpg 1920w" sizes="(max-width: 2560px) 100vw, 2560px" /> Les journalistes ont été rassurés de la présence effective des délégations des cinq pays. « Ce qui fait que tous les pays y compris le Burkina ont engagé leurs meilleurs athlètes, certains venus de l’extérieur pour affiner leur préparation à ces échéances » , a précisé Missiri Sawadogo. Le Burkina Faso qui a toujours dominé la compétition depuis son avènement en 2023 surtout dans les épreuves de haies et les épreuves techniques (sauts) sera représenté par 34 athlètes et cinq encadreurs. Ils seront menés par trois champions ou vice-champions d’Afrique de leurs catégories. Ce sont Zalissa Zongo, championne d’Afrique sortante de la catégorie U18 et championne de la région II récemment au Dakar avec des médailles dans les épreuves de longueur, triple saut et 100 m haies. Alan Sawadogo le meilleur junior au triple saut (plus de 16 m) et en longueur où il a obtenu la médaille d’or aux championnats juniors à Dakar en début juillet. Le 3 e est Abdoul Rachid Traoré dont le record national au triple est de 15,33 m et de 14’’58 pour les 110m haies. Voro KORAHIRE

items.title: Family of Apostle Kwadwo Safo Kantanka dismisses viral funeral date reports
items.description: The family of the late Apostle Prof. Dr Emeritus Kwadwo Safo Kantanka has dismissed reports circulating on social media claiming that a date has been set for his burial and final funeral rites. The family, in a statement issued after the reading of the will of the renowned Ghanaian inventor, said no official funeral date had been announced and urged the public to disregard unverified information making the rounds online. It said the clarification had become necessary due to growing misinformation surrounding arrangements for the funeral of the founder of the Kantanka Group. According to the family, consultations are still ongoing, and details of the burial and funeral programme will only be made public after all necessary processes have been completed. release (1) Download The family also distanced itself from various invitation cards, posters, flyers and social media posts circulating with proposed funeral dates, including July 30, July 31 and August 28, 2026. It stressed that none of the materials currently being shared has been authorised or issued by the family. “The Family has announced no official date for the burial and final funeral rites. The Family will communicate the approved funeral date and programme only after the necessary consultations have been concluded,” the statement said. Apostle Prof. Dr Emeritus Kwadwo Safo Kantanka, who was widely celebrated for his contributions to indigenous innovation, engineering and technology development in Ghana, died recently, leaving behind a legacy in local automobile manufacturing and invention.

items.title: Allemagne : prise d’otages dans une agence bancaire à Ratisbonne, au moins un mort
items.description: La situation demeurait confuse en fin d'apres-midi, ce 23 juillet, a Ratisbonne, dans le sud-est de l'Allemagne. D'apres les medias allemands, qui se montraient particulierement circonspects et citaient la police, une importante operation etait en cours dans cette ville bavaroise a la suite d'une prise d'otages dans une agence bancaire. Citant la meme source, le quotidien Tagesspiegel a rapporte que le forcene presume etait « vraisemblablement arme d'un couteau » et n'avait, pour l'heure, formule aucune revendication. « Pour l'instant, nous supposons que le suspect se trouve toujours a l'interieur du bâtiment », a indique Die Welt , citant le porte-parole de la police, Jonas Krauss. Selon la police, cette fois citee par le quotidien Bild , la prise d'otages aurait debute a 14 h 50. Une femme poignardee et brûlee vive a Stuttgart Les forces de l'ordre « supposent » qu'« au moins » une autre personne se trouve encore dans l'agence, a pour sa part rapporte la chaîne de television Tagesschau24. « Deux autres employes de la banque seraient toujours a l'interieur du bâtiment et se seraient barricades dans une piece », pouvait-on lire dans Bild . Une seule chose semblait plus ou moins certaine : une personne avait ete grievement blessee et transportee a l'hopital, ont rapporte plusieurs medias, citant les services d'urgence. Selon Bild , il s'agissait d'un employe de la banque. La victime a succombe a ses blessures a l'hopital, selon le media Heute . Sur les reseaux sociaux, l'effervescence etait palpable. L'annonce de cette prise d'otages est survenue quelques heures apres qu'une femme de 47 ans a ete poignardee et brulee vive dans un centre commercial situe au cœur de Stuttgart.

items.title: Bandits kill 23 farmers in fresh Zamfara attack
items.description: By Idris Salisu, Gusau At least 23 farmers have been killed by suspected bandits in an attack on farmlands in Sauna District, Talata Mafara Local Government Area of Zamfara State. Sources from the area told Vanguard on Thursday that the death toll rose from an initial 16 after seven additional bodies were recovered from nearby bushes. Residents said the victims were attacked while working on their farms on Thursday morning, with the assailants reportedly arriving on motorcycles and opening fire on farmers in the area. “Many others are still reported missing. The attackers came in groups and shot at farmers without warning,” a community source said. The remains of the 23 victims were later buried in a mass burial at the palace of the District Head of Sauna on Thursday, July 23, 2026. Sauna District and other parts of Talata Mafara Local Government Area have witnessed repeated attacks on farming communities, with residents frequently targeted while carrying out agricultural activities. Community leaders have called for increased security presence in rural areas to protect farmers during farming seasons. Confirming the incident, the Police Public Relations Officer of the Zamfara State Police Command, DSP Yazid Abubakar, said the Divisional Police Officer in Talata Mafara had reported the killing of 23 farmers in Sauna District. Yazid said the command had deployed additional personnel to the area and was working to track down the attackers. The post Bandits kill 23 farmers in fresh Zamfara attack appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Gov’t developing 1,200MW gas power plant to cut electricity costs – Ato Forson
items.description: Finance Minister Dr Cassiel Ato Forson has announced that the government is developing a 1,200-megawatt state-owned combined-cycle gas-fired power plant at Kafodzidzi-Abrobeano in the Komenda-Edina-Eguafo-Abrem Municipality to boost electricity generation and lower power costs. Presenting the 2026 Mid-Year Fiscal Policy Review in Parliament on Thursday, July 23, Dr Forson said feasibility studies have confirmed the project’s viability, with preparatory work already underway. “The government is also developing a 1,200-megawatt state-owned combined-cycle gas-fired power plant at Kafodzidzi-Abrobeano in the Komenda-Edina-Eguafo-Abrem Municipality.” He said environmental, engineering and permitting processes are progressing steadily, with the first phase of the project expected to come on stream in 2028. “Feasibility studies have confirmed the project’s viability, with environmental, engineering and permitting processes progressing steadily. The first 600-megawatt phase is expected to be commissioned in 2028.” The Finance Minister noted that the government has secured the gas turbines directly from GE Vernova to reduce the overall cost of the project. “To reduce costs, the government has secured the gas turbines directly from GE Vernova, achieving savings of between 35 and 45 percent compared with third-party procurement.” According to Dr Forson, the project will contribute to lowering the cost of electricity generation, reduce electricity tariffs, and create employment opportunities. “The project will lower electricity generation costs, help reduce electricity tariffs by 10-20 percent and create more than 2,000 direct and indirect jobs during the first phase.” He said the power plant forms part of the government’s broader efforts to strengthen the country’s energy infrastructure and ensure a more reliable and affordable electricity supply.

items.title: Nayala: 22 encadreurs de l’Immersion patriotique obligatoire formés pour mieux encadrer les nouveaux bacheliers
items.description: BURKINA-NAYALA-FORMATION-IMMERSION-PATRIOTIQUE-OBLIGATOIRE Nayala: 22 encadreurs de l’Immersion patriotique obligatoire formés pour mieux encadrer les nouveaux bacheliers Toma, le 21 juil. 2026 (AIB) – Le Haut-commissaire de la province du Nayala, Honoré Frédéric Paré, a lancé, mardi à Toma, la formation de 22 formateurs chargés d’encadrer les nouveaux bacheliers dans le cadre de l’Immersion patriotique obligatoire (IPO), prévue à partir du 27 juillet prochain. Les 22 formateurs qui assureront l’encadrement des nouveaux bacheliers de la province du Nayala dans le cadre de l’Immersion patriotique obligatoire (IPO) sont composés de 11 éléments des Forces de défense et de sécurité (FDS) et de 11 civils. Le Haut-commissaire a souligné que les formateurs auront pour mission de transmettre aussi bien des connaissances que des valeurs citoyennes. Il les a invités à faire preuve de rigueur, de discipline et d’exemplarité afin de garantir un encadrement de qualité. Le Directeur provincial de l’Enseignement post-primaire, secondaire, technique et professionnel du Nayala, Idrissa Roko, a rappelé que cette formation vise à préparer les encadreurs appelés à transmettre les valeurs de patriotisme, de civisme, de discipline et de responsabilité aux nouveaux bacheliers. Cette session constitue une étape essentielle dans les préparatifs de l’IPO 2026 et devrait permettre à la province de disposer d’encadreurs compétents pour accompagner efficacement la formation citoyenne des nouveaux bacheliers. Agence d'information du Burkina KTA/bak

items.title: Osun Election: Group rallies Accord, ADC, APC for peaceful poll
items.description: By Shina Abubakar, Osogbo A socio-political movement, General Gbasky Movement, has urged major political parties and stakeholders in Osun State to promote issue-based campaigns and ensure a peaceful governorship election on August 15. The group called on political parties, security agencies, religious leaders, traditional rulers, youth and women organisations to work together toward a violence-free electoral process. Speaking at a summit held at Leisure Spring Hotel Hall, Osogbo, with the theme, “Mobilising for Unity, Progress and Grassroots Development,” the Convener of the movement, Hon. Olufemi Aponbiede, said the gathering was aimed at fostering unity among stakeholders and promoting peace before, during and after the election. Aponbiede said elections are temporary, but the unity and development of communities should remain a lasting priority. “As we approach the forthcoming elections, this is the time for unity, not division. Elections will come and go, but our communities and relationships must remain strong. We must reject bitterness, violence and politics of hatred. Instead, let us embrace peace, mutual respect and constructive engagement,” he said. He stressed the movement’s commitment to people-centred leadership, noting that sustainable development begins at the grassroots through policies that improve the lives of citizens. The convener identified youth empowerment, women inclusion, support for farmers, entrepreneurship, improved education, healthcare and infrastructure as key areas requiring continued attention. “Our strength lies in our unity. When we stand together, no challenge is too great and no goal is beyond our reach,” he said. Aponbiede urged political actors across party lines to conduct peaceful campaigns and avoid actions capable of threatening the peace of the state. He also encouraged eligible voters to participate actively in the electoral process while respecting differing political views. “I call on every member here today to become an ambassador of peace. Let us campaign with dignity, respect opposing views and encourage every eligible citizen to participate peacefully in the electoral process,” he said. He assured that the General Gbasky Movement would continue to promote inclusion, transparency, accountability and sustainable grassroots development. Aponbiede expressed optimism that collaboration among political leaders, security agencies, religious institutions and community stakeholders would contribute to a peaceful election and the overall development of Osun State. The post Osun Election: Group rallies Accord, ADC, APC for peaceful poll appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: The mark of a democrat: Why Dr Bawumia is right to defend free speech over persecution and prosecution
items.description: In a volatile political climate often defined by animosity, insults and retaliation, former Vice-President and NPP Flagbearer Dr Mahamudu Bawumia has remarkably taken a different path. Yesterday, Dr Bawumia publicly called for the discontinuation of a prosecution initiated in his name against an NDC organiser in Agona West who had publicly insulted the former Vice-President. In his statement distancing himself from the prosecution, Dr Bawumia made it clear that he neither lodged a complaint nor supported the arrest of any Ghanaian over speech. At a time when speech is being criminalised through the persecution and prosecution of citizens by the state, Dr Bawumia’s decisive move is a defining moment of leadership — one that puts democratic values and national unity above personal grievance. It is for this reason that the former Vice-President, arguably the most verbally abused politician in recent times, is being hailed by true democrats and advocates of the free and responsible exercise of speech. One of them is respected journalist Manasseh Azure Awuni. “The credibility of Dr Bawumia’s statement lies in the fact that, as Vice-President, he was constantly abused and insulted for eight years without such arrests,” Manasseh wrote, as he praised both Dr Bawumia’s move and his track record of tolerance as a leader. Indeed, throughout his nearly two decades in public political life, Dr Bawumia and his wife have been no strangers to vicious criticism, baseless allegations, name-calling and despicable insults. Yet he has remained calm, never retaliated, and has remarkably preferred policy debates to retaliatory attacks. And as Vice-President for eight years, wielding power and authority as Chairman of the Police Council, he never, for once, used his position to arrest, persecute or prosecute those who constantly insulted him. In emphasising that his “conviction remains that the answer to offensive speech is better speech, and the answer to falsehood is truth, not handcuffs, courtrooms and crushing bail conditions imposed on ordinary Ghanaians”, Dr Bawumia is only being consistent with the tolerance he has demonstrated throughout his political life. It is a principle he is now asking the Ghana Police Service and the Attorney-General to uphold by discontinuing the prosecution and, to a large extent, the persecution of the young man. A call for a different kind of politics Dr Bawumia’s call goes beyond this case. By this remarkable demonstration of tolerance, Dr Bawumia is also calling for a different kind of politics to advance the kind of participatory democracy Ghana wants to build. As the NPP Flagbearer succinctly captured in his statement, “Ghana’s democracy is strengthened when citizens, including those who oppose us, can speak freely without fear.” Dr Bawumia has shown leadership and his human side by extending forgiveness to the young man at the centre of the case and urging all political actors to redirect their energy towards “the issues that matter to the Ghanaian people” — jobs, the cost of living, digital transformation and national development. Dr Bawumia’s timely intervention, in the midst of the unacceptable criminalisation of speech, must be a clear signal to supporters and opponents alike that Ghana’s democracy is bigger than any individual, and that freedom of expression must be protected even when it is uncomfortable. His call should also be a reminder to all those who facilitate and spread abuse against people that their right to free speech also requires them to respect the freedom and dignity of their opponents. Criticism and demands for accountability should not be turned into vicious insults, name-calling, baseless allegations and personal attacks. The message from the NPP Flagbearer is simple: let us respect citizens’ right to free speech; let us argue ideas and not jail people; let us compete on records, not on reprisals. Yes, citizens must be measured and responsible in their speech. But leaders in power now must not take the nation back to the dark, oppressive days of criminalising speech. In Dr Bawumia’s words, our democracy will be stronger and Ghana will be better for all.

items.title: Ghana to repay GH¢111 billion domestic debt over next two years – Finance Minister
items.description: Finance Minister Dr Cassiel Ato Forson says Ghana faces GH¢111 billion in domestic debt repayments over the next two years, describing the looming obligation as the country’s “true Agenda 111” and warning that government must plan to meet the commitments. Speaking during the presentation of the 2026 Mid-Year Fiscal Policy Review in Parliament on Thursday, Dr Forson said although Ghana has overcome what economists describe as the “original sin” by regaining the ability to borrow long-term in its own currency, the government has inherited significant debt obligations arising from the Domestic Debt Exchange Programme (DDEP). “While Ghana’s debt restructuring is nearly complete, prudent debt management requires us to look beyond today to tomorrow’s obligations,” he said. The Finance Minister disclosed that GH¢58 billion in DDEP bonds will mature in 2027, with a further GH¢53 billion falling due in 2028. “In two years alone, Ghana has to repay GH¢111 billion. One, one, one. Mr Speaker, this is the true Agenda 111. This is the true Agenda 111 Ghana must deal with,” he told Parliament. Dr Forson argued that the Domestic Debt Exchange Programme was designed to delay Ghana’s debt burden rather than eliminate it. “Because the Domestic Debt Exchange Programme was not designed to solve Ghana’s debt problems. It was designed to postpone it deliberately, knowingly, and cynically,” he said. Using a metaphor to illustrate his point, the Finance Minister said the architects of the programme failed to address the country’s underlying debt challenges. “It architected mortgaged tomorrow to survive today. They had a duty to fix the roof. Instead, they moved the leak to another room and declared the house repaired,” he stated. According to Dr Forson, the government has responded by strengthening the Sinking Fund to ensure the country is adequately prepared to meet the large debt repayments due over the next two years. “Meeting obligations of this magnitude requires advanced planning, not last-minute scrambling. This is precisely why this government has strengthened the Sinking Fund to set aside money to repay future debt obligations that we inherited,” he said. He announced that under the 2026–2029 Medium-Term Debt Management Strategy, seven per cent of non-oil tax revenue, together with proceeds from domestic bond issuances, will be paid into the Sinking Fund to build what he described as a “war chest” for the 2027 and 2028 debt maturities. Dr Forson further disclosed that the Sinking Fund had accumulated GH¢15.6 billion as of July 22, 2026, and expressed confidence that the balance would reach GH¢30 billion by the end of the year. “I’m proud to report that as of 22nd July, the war chest holds GH¢15.6 billion. We are on course to accumulate GH¢30 billion in the Sinking Fund by the end of 2026,” he said. According to the Finance Minister, the projected amount would be sufficient to settle the GH¢30 billion in DDEP debt scheduled to mature in February 2027, reducing refinancing risks and strengthening investor confidence. “This will be enough to repay the GH¢30 billion Domestic Debt Exchange Programme debt that will fall due in February 2027,” he noted. Dr Forson said the government’s strategy demonstrates a commitment to responsible fiscal management and ensuring that Ghana meets its future debt obligations without resorting to emergency measures. “Brick by brick, cedi by cedi, we are building the wall that will meet the wind so that when 2027 and 2028 come, Ghana will not scramble. Ghana will simply pay back this debt. This is the signal the Sinking Fund sends to investors, credit rating agencies and the people of Ghana, making it clear that this government plans ahead, honours its commitment, and manages the public finances better,” he told Parliament.

items.title: US House passes symbolic Iran war rebuke to Trump
items.description: The US House of Representatives passed a largely symbolic resolution Thursday calling for President Donald Trump to halt military action against Iran, delivering another congressional rebuke as the Middle East war sharply escalates. But as a concurrent resolution, it does not go to the president for signature and carries disputed legal force, meaning Trump is almost certain to ignore it.

items.title: Trump stuns Saudis by pinning nuclear deal to Israel ties
items.description: US President Donald Trump said Thursday that a nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia depends on the kingdom recognizing Israel -- throwing the agreement into turmoil just the day after it was signed. The White House insisted that the Saudis knew the deal was contingent on recognizing Israel, despite the fact that it was not mentioned when the deal was announced.
