items.title: Liga : Karim Adeyemi s'engage avec le Barça jusqu'en 2031
items.description: Le FC Barcelone a annoncé, jeudi, le recrutement de l'attaquant international allemand Karim Adeyemi, en provenance du Borussia Dortmund, avec lequel il s'est engagé jusqu'au 30 juin 2031. « Le FC Barcelone et le Borussia Dortmund sont parvenus à un accord pour le transfert de l'attaquant Karim Adeyemi, qui s'engage avec le club jusqu'au 30 juin 2031 » , indique le club catalan dans un communiqué. Le montant du transfert s'élèverait, selon la presse espagnole, à 29 millions d'euros. Âgé de 24 ans, Adeyemi évolue principalement au poste d'ailier, tout en étant capable de jouer sur les deux flancs de l'attaque ou en pointe. Son recrutement s'inscrit dans les choix de l'entraîneur Hansi Flick, qui l'avait lancé en sélection allemande alors qu'il n'avait que 19 ans. Après quatre saisons passées sous les couleurs du Borussia Dortmund, l'international allemand est appelé à renforcer le secteur offensif du Barça. Évoluant de préférence sur le côté droit, il devrait constituer une alternative à Lamine Yamal. Karim Adeyemi devient ainsi la deuxième recrue du FC Barcelone en vue de la prochaine saison, après l'arrivée de l'ailier britannique Anthony Gordon, en provenance de Newcastle United. The post Liga : Karim Adeyemi s'engage avec le Barça jusqu'en 2031 appeared first on Hespress Français - Actualités du Maroc .

items.title: Le pétrole dépasse les 100 dollars après des attaques en mer Rouge
items.description: Le baril de Brent a dépassé les 100 dollars jeudi, le conflit au Moyen-Orient semblant s'étendre en mer Rouge après la revendication par les rebelles houthis du Yémen d'attaques visant des pétroliers saoudiens dans la zone. Vers 13H40 GMT (15H40 à Paris), le prix du baril de Brent de la mer du Nord, pour livraison en septembre, prenait 5,84% à 99,56 dollars, après avoir dépassé les 100 dollars, son plus haut niveau depuis le mois de mai. Son équivalent américain, le baril de West Texas Intermediate, pour livraison le même mois, gagnait 4,68% à 90,89 dollars. Les Houthis – qui avaient annoncé lundi un blocus des ports saoudiens – ont revendiqué jeudi « une opération militaire » contre « deux pétroliers saoudiens », identifiant les navires comme l'Encelia et le Layla. Donald Trump a réagi en menaçant les rebelles houthis du Yémen et l'Iran d'une « punition militaire majeure » dans une publication sur son réseau Truth Social jeudi. Le risque d'un blocage du détroit de Bab el-Mandeb au large des côtes du Yémen est un facteur de hausse supplémentaire pour le cours du pétrole. La circulation maritime via la mer Rouge est devenue cruciale pour l'Arabie saoudite, car depuis le début de la guerre le royaume a considérablement augmenté ses exportations d'or noir via le port de Yanbu, sur la côte ouest du pays, pour contourner le blocage iranien du détroit d'Ormuz. Le volume de pétrole exporté par ce port correspond désormais à 75% du total des exportations de l'Arabie saoudite en temps normal, indique John Evans de PVM. Ces attaques fragilisent donc l'acheminement de ce pétrole vers l'Asie. « Le trajet entre Yanbu et la Chine prend un peu plus de 20 jours » en passant au large du Yémen, alors que remonter par le canal de Suez et contourner l'Afrique, implique un temps de voyage « de plus de 50 jours », explique l'analyste de PVM. Les tensions se poursuivent aussi du côté du détroit d'Ormuz. Les Gardiens de la Révolution iraniens ont annoncé jeudi avoir stoppé trois pétroliers qui tentaient de franchir le détroit, sans préciser à quel moment. Selon la société de données maritime Lloyd's List Intelligence, « il y a eu une baisse de 57% » du trafic maritime dans le détroit d'Ormuz la semaine dernière (achevée le 19 juillet) par rapport à celle précédente (achevée le 12 juillet). The post Le pétrole dépasse les 100 dollars après des attaques en mer Rouge appeared first on Hespress Français - Actualités du Maroc .

items.title: TPI de Kaloum : deux femmes condamnées pour maltraitance sur une enfant
items.description: Deux femmes, du nom d’Anna Lamah et de Fanta Touré, ont été fixées sur leur sort ce jeudi 23 juillet 2026 par le tribunal correctionnel de Kaloum. Elles étaient poursuivies pour des faits de « traite des êtres humains » et de maltraitance sur une enfant mineure. Les prévenues étaient poursuivies par le ministère public pour des faits de traite des êtres humains et de maltraitance sur la mineure Marie Kolié. Elles avaient été placées sous mandat de dépôt depuis le 29 juin 2026. Dans son verdict rendu à l’audience du jour, le tribunal présidé par Alpha Diaby a déclaré Anna Lamah coupable des faits de traite des êtres humains et de maltraitance. Au titre de la répression, elle a été condamnée à deux ans d’emprisonnement assortis de sursis et au paiement d’une amende de 10 millions de francs guinéens. Quant à Fanta Touré, elle a été reconnue coupable des faits de maltraitance. Elle écope d’un an d’emprisonnement assorti de sursis et d’une amende de 5 millions de francs guinéens. Par ailleurs, les deux femmes ont été condamnées solidairement à verser la somme de 40 millions de francs guinéens à la petite Marie Kolié au titre des dommages et intérêts pour le préjudice subi. Elisa CAMARA L’article TPI de Kaloum : deux femmes condamnées pour maltraitance sur une enfant est apparu en premier sur Mediaguinee.com .

items.title: Africa must increase domestic health financing to end preventable maternal deaths – UNFPA
items.description: The Regional Director of the United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA) for West and Central Africa, Dr Sennen Hounton, has called on African governments to increase domestic investment in healthcare, warning that inadequate and unsustainable financing remains a major obstacle to ending preventable maternal deaths across the continent. Speaking during a press conference on the sidelines of the African Union Extraordinary Summit on Health Financing in Accra on Wednesday, July 23, Dr Hounton said governments have demonstrated political commitment to tackling maternal mortality, but persistent funding gaps and weaknesses within health systems continue to undermine progress. “What is making it difficult for countries to achieve the target of maternal mortality elimination? It’s not lack of government will,” he said. “In fact, in many countries you will see governments having even a presidential initiative to address that topic. But it’s a set of factors that is making it difficult.” According to him, many women, particularly those living in rural and underserved communities, still lack access to quality maternal healthcare because health facilities and skilled birth attendants remain concentrated in urban areas. “Not all women are still able to access quality and life-saving interventions. In many of our countries we have facilities in urban areas and we don’t have facilities covering the rural areas. We have midwives mainly in the cities and we don’t have midwives in the peripheral part of the countries,” he noted. Dr Hounton stressed that reducing maternal mortality should no longer be viewed solely as the responsibility of ministries of health, but rather as a broader national development issue requiring coordinated action across government. “We thought at UNFPA that it is important to move the conversation from a health sector perspective to a development multi-sectoral perspective,” he said. “For far too long, we thought that maternal mortality and newborn mortality are just for the health sector. But we realised that even to have the midwives, the Ministry of Finance and Budget needs not only to appropriate money to recruit, but release this money so we can recruit and deploy.” He explained that this thinking informed the African Union summit’s focus on domestic and sustainable financing for health. “The financing is not that we didn’t know it was important, but it is more important and that’s why the topic of this AU Extraordinary Summit is domestic financing and sustainable financing,” he added. The UNFPA Regional Director also called for stronger accountability mechanisms, urging governments to regularly review maternal health data and assess whether interventions are delivering results. “We only make progress on what we measure,” he said. “Annually or semi-annually, whether it is at the President’s Cabinet level or at the decentralised level of government, we need to sit down and look at the data. Are we making progress? Are we not making progress? And if we are not, what do we need to do?” Dr Hounton further highlighted the importance of expanding health insurance coverage and providing targeted support for poor and vulnerable pregnant women who remain excluded from social protection schemes. “Many people are still not part of this social safety net, or the cost is just too much for them,” he observed. “We need to identify the extreme poor, those who, despite the national insurance, cannot pay the premium. We must find solutions to address that. Otherwise, they will continue to drive the number of maternal mortality.” While acknowledging that African governments face competing demands for limited public resources, he said innovative financing mechanisms were needed to bridge the gap. Dr Hounton revealed that UNFPA is working with the African Development Bank to establish financing instruments combining concessional loans and grants to enable countries to invest more in healthcare. “Countries must have access to capital and resources to do the investment that they are doing,” he said. “What we are doing with the African Development Bank is creating financing instruments. It’s a combination of concessional loans and grants, and countries can then have access and invest in the health of their communities.” He maintained that all future investments in healthcare must prioritise equity to ensure people living in remote communities enjoy the same access to quality healthcare as those in major cities. “As you raise capital, you need to be thinking about equity… those who are not only living in cities like you and I here, but those who are in the remote area of our countries and need also to enjoy full and dignified health,” he said. Despite the challenges, Dr Hounton expressed confidence that African countries could significantly reduce maternal mortality if governments strengthened domestic financing, improved accountability and ensured equitable access to quality healthcare services.

items.title: Reps move to establish Federal College of Fisheries Technology in Rivers
items.description: By Gift Chapi Odekina, Abuja The House of Representatives Committee on Ocean and Fisheries has recommended the passage of a bill seeking to establish the Federal College of Fisheries Technology and Marine Engineering in Ngo, Andoni Local Government Area of Rivers State. Presenting the committee’s report to the House, Chairman of the Committee, Hon. Akarachi Etinosa Amadi, said the proposed legislation seeks to amend the Agricultural Research Council of Nigeria Act, Cap A12, Laws of the Federation of Nigeria, 2004, to provide the legal framework for the establishment of the specialised institution. Amadi recalled that the House had, on May 7, 2025, concluded debate on the general principles of the bill before referring it to the committee for further legislative action. According to him, the proposed college is aimed at strengthening Nigeria’s blue economy by providing specialised training in fisheries technology, aquaculture and marine engineering. He explained that locating the institution in the riverine community of Ngo would help address the shortage of skilled manpower in the maritime sector, expand access to technical and vocational education, and stimulate economic development across the Niger Delta. The committee chairman disclosed that a public hearing on the bill was held on July 14, 2025, during which stakeholders from the maritime, education and regional development sectors expressed overwhelming support for the proposal. He said participants unanimously endorsed the bill and urged its passage, describing it as a strategic intervention that would enhance capacity building and drive sustainable growth in Nigeria’s coastal communities. Amadi further explained that the bill contains three clauses, with the first two providing the legal basis for amending the Agricultural Research Council of Nigeria Act. He noted that the third clause is the substantive provision of the legislation, as it inserts the proposed Federal College of Fisheries Technology and Marine Engineering into the Third Schedule of the principal Act alongside other federal institutions. According to him, the inclusion of the college in the schedule would confer statutory recognition on the institution, enabling it to access federal funding and fully discharge its mandate in fisheries technology and marine engineering. Following its review, the committee retained all the clauses of the bill without amendment and recommended that the House consider and pass the legislation as presented. The post Reps move to establish Federal College of Fisheries Technology in Rivers appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: La FBF renforce les mécanismes de protection et de valorisation de son nouveau logo
items.description: FOOT-BFA-SPORT-FBF-PROTECTION-LOGO La FBF renforce les mécanismes de protection et de valorisation de son nouveau logo Ouagadougou, 23 juil. 2026 (AIB) – La Fédération burkinabè de football (FBF) a tenu, mercredi à Ouagadougou, un atelier de réflexion consacré à la mise en place d'un dispositif de protection et de valorisation de son nouveau logo, dans le cadre de sa politique de modernisation et de consolidation de son identité institutionnelle. Cette rencontre vise à définir un cadre stratégique garantissant une exploitation optimale de ce symbole, considéré comme un actif majeur de la Fédération. Les échanges portent sur six axes prioritaires : la protection juridique et institutionnelle du logo, l'élaboration d'un modèle économique et commercial, la lutte contre la contrefaçon et les usages illicites, la définition d'un cahier des charges destiné à l'équipementier Tovio, la mise en place de mécanismes de gouvernance et de contrôle, ainsi que la communication et la sensibilisation des différents acteurs. Selon le président de la commission de réflexion et troisième vice-président de la FBF, Kassoum Traoré, le nouveau logo incarne bien plus qu'une identité visuelle. « Il traduit les valeurs, l'histoire et les ambitions de notre Fédération. Sa protection juridique et institutionnelle est une exigence afin d'en préserver l'intégrité et d'en garantir une utilisation conforme aux intérêts de la FBF », a-t-il souligné. Présidant les travaux, le président de la FBF, Oumarou Sawadogo, a insisté sur les enjeux identitaires et économiques liés à cette initiative. Il a relevé que de nombreux maillots portant l’emblème de la Fédération sont actuellement commercialisés sans qu'aucune retombée financière ne profite à l'institution. « L'objectif est de clarifier, avec notre équipementier, les domaines de compétence de chaque partie afin de garantir une exploitation organisée et bénéfique pour tous », a-t-il déclaré. Le président de la FBF a également indiqué que l'institution travaille en étroite collaboration avec la Coordination nationale de lutte contre la fraude, les services des Douanes, la Gendarmerie nationale et la Police nationale pour endiguer la commercialisation des produits contrefaits. Toutefois, il a précisé qu'aucune opération de répression immédiate ne sera engagée avant les conclusions de la commission, dont les recommandations détermineront les modalités d'application des futures mesures. Agence d'information du Burkina as/ata

items.title: Gov’t to introduce VAT Reward Scheme for consumers who demand invoices – Ato Forson
items.description: Finance Minister Dr Cassiel Ato Forson has announced that the government will introduce a VAT Reward Scheme aimed at encouraging greater public participation in tax compliance and protecting the country’s tax base. Presenting the 2026 Mid-Year Fiscal Policy Review in Parliament on Thursday, Dr Forson said the initiative would reward consumers who request and obtain valid VAT invoices when making purchases. According to the Finance Minister, customers who obtain valid VAT invoices will qualify for periodic rewards under the scheme, providing an incentive for citizens to actively participate in strengthening tax compliance. READ ALSO: Abolishing taxes did not reduce revenue; Ghana collected more in 2025 – Ato Forson “To encourage participation and improve compliance, the government will also introduce the VAT Reward Scheme under which customers who obtain valid VAT invoices will qualify for periodic rewards,” he announced. Dr Forson said the initiative is designed to involve every Ghanaian in safeguarding the nation’s tax system by promoting the consistent demand for VAT invoices during commercial transactions. “This will actively involve every Ghanaian in protecting the nation’s tax base,” he told Parliament. He stressed that the success of the country’s tax system depends not only on enforcement measures but also on building a culture of voluntary compliance through public engagement. “This is how a tax culture is built: not through fear, but through participation, transparency, and reward,” the finance minister stated. The proposed VAT Reward Scheme forms part of the government’s broader efforts to improve domestic revenue mobilisation, strengthen tax compliance, and enhance public confidence in the country’s tax administration.

items.title: Burkina Faso : La promotion 1999-2001 de l’École nationale de Police célèbre ses 25 ans de service
items.description: La cérémonie de lancement des 72 heures de commémoration du jubilé d'argent de la promotion 1999-2001 de l'École nationale de police (ENP) s'est tenue ce 23 juillet 2026, à Ouagadougou. Ces festivités, qui se tiennent jusqu’au 25 juillet, sont placées sous le thème évocateur : « Servir avec honneur : 25 ans de dévouement et de sacrifices au service de l’État et de la nation ». Baptisée « Professionnalisme-Action-Efficacité », la 57ᵉ promotion était initialement composée de 556 éléments de la Police nationale, 7 de la Police municipale et 92 de la Garde de sécurité pénitentiaire (GSP). Au fil des ans, les efforts de nombreux agents ont été récompensés par de belles ascensions au sein de la hiérarchie. Au sein de la Police nationale, 15 commissaires de police sont aujourd’hui parvenus au grade d’inspecteurs généraux. Sur les 436 élèves agents de police, 11 sont devenus commissaires, dont 7 commissaires principaux. Par ailleurs, 95 agents ont accédé au corps des officiers, et 276 portent désormais le grade d’adjudant-chef de police. Du côté de la Garde de sécurité pénitentiaire, plusieurs camarades sont promus inspecteurs, contrôleurs et assistants, tandis que la Police municipale compte à présent 7 cadres issus de cette promotion, parmi lesquels 4 inspecteurs, 2 assistants et 1 agent occupant d’importantes fonctions. Au-delà des chiffres, cette célébration est l’occasion de saluer le savoir-faire de ces agents, leur résilience face aux épreuves traversées par la nation, ainsi que leur engagement social, qui illustre l’esprit de corps bien au-delà du cadre professionnel. La promotion 1999-2001 de l’ENP célèbre 25 ans de service et de sacrifices -©Faso7 Cependant, ce quart de siècle de service a aussi été marqué par le sacrifice suprême. 57 agents sont tombés au champ d’honneur, dont 49 de la Police nationale et 8 éléments de la GSP. « Nous avons mouillé le maillot » « ​Durant ces 25 ans, nous sommes fiers car beaucoup ont pu remplir quand même les rêves qu'ils avaient depuis l'école. Nous avons occupé les plus hautes marches vraiment de l'administration. Au niveau international, nous avons vraiment mouillé le maillot, et actuellement, la MONUSCO est gérée par une de notre promotion » , a indiqué l’inspecteur général de Police Emmanuel Kaboré, président du comité d’organisation. Le commissaire divisionnaire de Police Wendinmanegdé Emmanuel Zongo, directeur de cabinet du ministre de la Sécurité, a pour sa part exprimé la reconnaissance de l’institution pour la contribution de la promotion 57 à la sécurité et à la stabilité du pays. Il a rendu hommage à l’ensemble des forces de la Police nationale, Police municipale et GSP avant d’inviter les membres à maintenir ce niveau d’excellence et à inspirer les nouvelles générations face aux défis sécuritaires actuels. « Cette promotion constitue une fierté pour toute la Police, la GSP et naturellement la Police municipale, pour les cadres et pour tout ce qu’ils ont accompli. Elle est aujourd’hui une référence, et nous souhaitons que les jeunes générations puissent s’en inspirer pour travailler davantage pour notre Nation et répondre aux attentes des populations sur les différents théâtres d’opérations » , a-t-il déclaré. Placé sous le parrainage de Yéro Boly, ancien ministre de la Défense, ce jubilé a été l’occasion pour celui-ci d’appeler la promotion à renforcer la solidarité tant entre ses membres qu’avec les collaborateurs sur le terrain. Il a souligné que les épreuves à venir restent exigeantes et requièrent un engagement sans faille pour poursuivre la défense du territoire et la sécurité des citoyens, dans un contexte sous-régional particulièrement difficile. Ces festivités sont placées sous le thème évocateur : « Servir avec honneur : 25 ans de dévouement et de sacrifices au service de l’État et de la nation » -©Faso7 « Je n’ai pas manqué de leur dire que ce qui les attendait restait coriace, du point de vue des efforts à fournir et de l’engagement sans faille qu’ils doivent garder pour défendre le territoire, défendre la patrie et sécuriser les citoyens », a-t-il insisté. Plusieurs activités ponctueront ces festivités, notamment des panels, cross populaire, séance d’aérobic, match de gala et une soirée d’hommage en mémoire des disparus. Par ailleurs, la promotion 1999-2001 a remis des vivres aux orphelins de promotionnaires défunts, effectué des dons à Faso mêbo et participé au reboisement de plus de 300 arbres. Lazard KOLA Faso7 L’article Burkina Faso : La promotion 1999-2001 de l'École nationale de Police célèbre ses 25 ans de service est apparu en premier sur Faso7 .

items.title: Mort d'Abderrahim Fqir : la police italienne défend son intervention, l'enquête se poursuit
items.description: L'affaire de la mort d'Abderrahim Fqir continue d'alimenter le débat en Italie et vire aux règlements de comptes politiques. Alors que les circonstances du décès de ce ressortissant marocain lors d'une intervention policière à Bologne font l'objet d'une enquête judiciaire, le principal syndicat de la police italienne est monté au créneau pour défendre les agents impliqués, tout en condamnant les violences qui ont éclaté lors des manifestations organisées après le drame. Le Syndicat italien unifié des travailleurs de la police (SIULP) a annoncé s'être constitué partie civile dans la procédure pénale ouverte à la suite des affrontements ayant suivi le décès du jeune Marocain. Selon l'organisation, ces incidents ont fait plusieurs dizaines de blessés parmi les forces de l'ordre et provoqué d'importants dégâts matériels. Dans un communiqué, la branche du syndicat à Bologne indique qu'elle assurera gratuitement la défense juridique des policiers blessés et accompagnera leurs démarches afin d'obtenir réparation pour les préjudices physiques, moraux et économiques subis. Le SIULP affirme que cette initiative vise à défendre « la dignité des femmes et des hommes en uniforme » et à lutter contre « toute forme de violence organisée ». Le syndicat dénonce un climat hostile Le secrétaire provincial du syndicat à Bologne, Pasquale Palma, a présenté ses condoléances à la famille d'Abderrahim Fqir, tout en exprimant sa confiance dans le travail de la justice italienne. « Je tiens à adresser à la famille d'Abderrahim Fqir mes plus sincères condoléances, avec la conviction que la justice fera toute la lumière sur cette affaire », a-t-il déclaré. Il estime toutefois que le climat ayant précédé les manifestations a contribué à alimenter une hostilité croissante envers les forces de l'ordre. « Le contexte qui a précédé et accompagné cette manifestation, soutenu également par certains responsables institutionnels, a alimenté de manière irresponsable l'hostilité envers la police et favorisé des épisodes de violence et de destruction », a-t-il affirmé. Selon lui, « remettre en cause la légitimité des forces de l'ordre revient à affaiblir l'État et à laisser le champ libre à ceux qui privilégient la violence plutôt que le dialogue, fondement de toute démocratie » . Une intervention jugée « proportionnée » Au niveau national, le SIULP défend également l'action des policiers impliqués dans l'intervention qui a précédé le décès du ressortissant marocain. Son secrétaire général, Felice Romano, rappelle que les agents sont intervenus après plusieurs appels d'urgence signalant « une personne en état d'extrême agitation, agressant des passants et dégradant des biens ». « Face à la perte d'une vie humaine, nos premières pensées vont à la douleur de la famille. Toute vie perdue est une perte pour l'ensemble de la société », a-t-il déclaré. Tout en exprimant cette compassion, le responsable syndical affirme toutefois avoir « une confiance totale dans le professionnalisme et la légitimité de l'action des policiers ». Selon lui, l'intervention s'est déroulée « dans le respect du principe de proportionnalité » . Il souligne notamment que les agents, bien qu'équipés de pistolets à impulsion électrique, ont choisi d'utiliser uniquement du gaz lacrymogène au poivre, en présence des secours et avec l'aide de plusieurs citoyens ayant participé à la maîtrise de la victime. Le syndicat critique par ailleurs les réactions politiques ayant suivi le drame, estimant que certains responsables cherchent davantage à exploiter l'affaire qu'à apaiser les tensions. « Il est particulièrement grave que certains semblent alimenter la confrontation au lieu de contribuer à faire la lumière sur les faits, au risque d'encourager des tensions institutionnelles », a estimé Felice Romano. Le responsable syndical assure enfin que les policiers concernés se sont immédiatement mis à la disposition de la justice et appelle à laisser les magistrats établir les faits, plutôt que de tirer des conclusions à partir « de vidéos sorties de leur contexte et diffusées sur les réseaux sociaux ». Une affaire sous haute tension La mort d'Abderrahim Fqir est devenue une affaire particulièrement sensible en Italie. Les images de son interpellation ont suscité une vive émotion et relancé le débat sur l'usage de la force par les forces de l'ordre. Les autorités judiciaires de Bologne poursuivent leurs investigations afin de déterminer les circonstances exactes du décès, tandis que le Maroc, par la voix du ministère des Affaires étrangères, a appelé les autorités italiennes à mener une enquête complète afin d'établir les responsabilités. The post Mort d'Abderrahim Fqir : la police italienne défend son intervention, l'enquête se poursuit appeared first on Hespress Français - Actualités du Maroc .

items.title: Cohésion sociale au Burkina Faso : Cheikh Tang Zoug Moré s’engage pour la paix et le vivre-ensemble
items.description: Dans un contexte national marqué par des défis sécuritaires et la nécessité de renforcer le vivre-ensemble entre les communautés, certaines personnalités œuvrent au rapprochement des populations. Parmi elles figure Redwane Pougda, plus connu sous le nom de Cheikh Tang Zoug Moré, qui incarne cette vision d’une foi tournée vers le dialogue et la solidarité. Ce 22 juillet 2026, à Ouagadougou, il a présenté ses actions en faveur de la promotion de la paix et de la cohésion sociale. Pour Cheikh Tang Zoug Moré, la religion doit rassembler plutôt que de diviser. Son action s'articule autour du dialogue intercommunautaire, du renforcement de la cohésion sociale et de la lutte contre le sectarisme. Convaincu que tous les hommes sont égaux devant Dieu, il invite chaque citoyen à devenir un artisan de paix au quotidien, de la sphère familiale et à l'échelle nationale. Rappelant sans cesse que le jugement appartient à Dieu seul et que tous les hommes sont égaux, Cheikh Tang Zoug Moré exhorte chacun à devenir un artisan de paix dans son foyer, son quartier et son pays. « Aucune religion ne peut justifier la haine. La foi doit rapprocher les hommes plutôt que les opposer. Tous les êtres humains sont égaux devant Dieu, et le jugement appartient uniquement au Créateur » , a-t-il souligné. Au-delà de sa fonction de guide religieux, Cheikh Tang Zoug Moré se considère comme un serviteur de la communauté. Son engagement quotidien se traduit par l’écoute des personnes en difficulté, le conseil aux familles, l’accompagnement des jeunes et la promotion des valeurs morales. Pour lui, le véritable leadership consiste à servir les autres avec humilité. À travers son association « Djamiyatoul Filoul Kayraat », Cheikh Tang Zoug Moré multiplie les initiatives pour consolider le vivre-ensemble sur le territoire. Il agit sur plusieurs fronts notamment la sensibilisation à la paix, la promotion du dialogue interreligieux, la solidarité envers les personnes vulnérables, ainsi que l'encadrement des jeunes et l'éducation à la citoyenneté. Son ambition ultime est de participer à l'édification d'une société burkinabè plus juste, plus fraternelle et plus solidaire. « Les différences religieuses ne doivent jamais devenir des motifs de conflits, mais des occasions de mieux se connaître. La véritable foi se mesure à travers les actes de solidarité, de respect et d'amour envers les autres » , a-t-il déclaré. Faso7 L’article Cohésion sociale au Burkina Faso : Cheikh Tang Zoug Moré s'engage pour la paix et le vivre-ensemble est apparu en premier sur Faso7 .

items.title: Haaland et Yamal, joueurs les plus chers de l’histoire du football (classement)
items.description: Erling Haaland et Lamine Yamal deviennent officiellement les joueurs les plus chers de l'histoire du football, avec une valeur marchande record de 220 millions d'euros chacun, selon la dernière mise à jour de Transfermarkt publiée au lendemain du sacre de l'Espagne au Mondial 2026. Le mercato d'été n'aura pas attendu les transferts pour produire son lot de records. Selon la dernière mise à jour de Transfermarkt, publiée au lendemain de la Coupe du monde 2026, Erling Haaland et Lamine Yamal deviennent officiellement les joueurs les plus chers de l'histoire du football, avec une valeur marchande de 220 millions d'euros chacun; un montant jamais atteint jusque-là sur la plateforme allemande. Un Mondial qui a dopé les valeurs Ce nouveau classement n'a rien d'anodin : il intervient directement après le sacre de l'Espagne, et Transfermarkt en tire les conséquences sur la cote de 99 joueurs ayant marqué le tournoi. Comme le veut la tradition de la plateforme, aucune baisse n'a été enregistrée, seuls les joueurs performants ont vu leur valeur grimper. Erling Haaland en est le grand symbole. Auteur de 7 buts en seulement 18 tirs, meilleur taux de conversion pour un joueur ayant tenté plus de 15 frappes depuis Gary Lineker en 1986, le Norvégien a porté une sélection qui n'est pas coutumière des grands tournois jusqu'en quarts de finale, où l'aventure s'est arrêtée face à l'Angleterre (2-1 a.p.). Une performance individuelle XXL qui a suffi à propulser son prix à un niveau inédit, malgré l'élimination précoce de la Norvège. Lamine Yamal complète ce duo record. Pilier du jeu espagnol aux côtés de Rodri, Pau Cubarsí et Unai Simon, l'ailier barcelonais a contribué à la conquête du deuxième titre mondial de la Roja, décroché face à l'Argentine en finale. Sa valeur grimpe elle aussi à 220 millions d'euros, une reconnaissance de son rôle dans le sacre espagnol autant que de son potentiel, à seulement 19 ans. Kylian Mbappé, meilleur buteur du tournoi avec 10 réalisations, complète le podium à 200 millions d'euros, conservant son statut de référence offensive mondiale malgré la défaite française en cours de compétition. Michael Olise, élu meilleur passeur du Mondial avec 7 offrandes décisives, voit également sa cote grimper à 170 millions d'euros. Jude Bellingham, enfin, ferme ce quintette de tête à 160 millions d'euros (+30 M€), auteur de 6 buts qui égalent le record historique d'un joueur anglais lors d'une Coupe du monde, dont un doublé décisif face à la Norvège en quarts de finale. Comment Transfermarkt fixe ses valeurs Contrairement à une idée reçue, Transfermarkt ne s'appuie pas sur un algorithme automatisé mais sur une méthodologie de crowdsourcing, mêlant expertise interne et contribution d'une communauté de passionnés répartis en « Data Scouts » à travers le monde. Plusieurs critères entrent en ligne de compte : l'âge du joueur et son potentiel de progression, ses performances sportives individuelles et collectives, la durée restante de son contrat, sa réputation et sa notoriété internationale, ainsi que les tendances observées sur le marché des transferts. Les statistiques brutes, buts, passes décisives, temps de jeu pèsent également dans la balance, sans toutefois suffire à elles seules : le montant final reste, selon les propres mots de la plateforme, le résultat d'un arbitrage humain plutôt que d'un simple calcul automatique. Top 10 Transfermarkt : Erling Haaland (220 M€) – Lamine Yamal (220 M€) – Kylian Mbappé (200 M€) – Michael Olise (170 M€) – Jude Bellingham (160 M€) – Pedri (150 M€) – Vinicius Jr. (140 M€) – Vitinha (140 M€) – Khvicha Kvaratskhelia (140 M€) – João Neves (140 M€)

items.title: Reps minority caucus walks out of plenary over Tinubu’s state police bill
items.description: …as 311 Reps vote to pass Bill By Gift Chapi Odekina, Abuja The Minority Caucus in the House of Representatives on Thursday staged a walkout from the chamber in protest over the passage of the constitutional amendment bill seeking to establish state police, accusing the House leadership of violating constitutional provisions and the chamber’s Standing Orders. Addressing journalists shortly after leaving the chamber, the Minority Leader, Fredrick Agbedi, alongside other caucus leaders, insisted that their action was not borne out of opposition to state police but against what they described as the abuse of parliamentary procedures. Speaking on behalf of the caucus, Agbedi said the Speaker, while presiding as Chairman of the Committee of the Whole, ignored clear constitutional requirements governing the alteration of the Constitution. He argued that attendance in the House is determined through members’ registers signed on each legislative day and not by names read out during proceedings. “The Constitution is the grand norm. It is the rule book from which even our Standing Orders derive their powers. For the Speaker, acting as Chairman of the Committee of the Whole, to disregard the clear provisions of Section 9(2) of the 1999 Constitution, as amended, is a great affront to the workings of the House of Representatives,” he said. The caucus maintained that constitutional amendment bills must be considered clause by clause, with each provision supported by a two-thirds majority of members as stipulated in both the Constitution and the House Standing Orders. Quoting Order 13 Rule 15(a) of the House Rules, the lawmakers said, “In the clause-by-clause consideration in the Committee of the Whole, a clause shall be deemed passed only if supported by a positive vote of two-thirds majority of members of the House.” They faulted the decision to merely read the long title of the bill and determine its passage through a voice vote without detailed consideration of each clause. “We are not against the creation of state police,” the caucus stressed. “Whatever will help address insecurity in Nigeria, we are prepared to support. But every action taken by Parliament must follow due process.” According to the lawmakers, they had earlier met with the Speaker and discussed proposed amendments to the bill, with the Speaker reportedly acknowledging areas where changes could be accommodated. “Mr. Speaker even identified areas where we could intervene, but when he sat as Chairman of the Committee of the Whole, he simply read the long title and put the question through a voice vote. That is absurd,” they said. The Minority Caucus warned that failure to comply with constitutional procedures undermines the credibility of the National Assembly and Nigeria’s democratic institutions. “When the rules and regulations are not complied with, it affects the credibility of the institution as well as the credibility of Nigeria and our democracy,” the lawmakers said. They further alleged that the Speaker denied them the opportunity to raise constitutional points of order before proceeding with the vote. “As Minority Leader, I raised a point of order, but before hearing me, the Chairman ruled me out of order. Which order was he ruling me out of? He ought to have heard my point of order first before deciding whether it complied with our rules or the Constitution,” the caucus stated. The lawmakers also questioned how the constitutional amendment could validly secure the constitutionally required two-thirds majority after opposition members had walked out of the chamber. “If we walked out of the House, where are our votes? Can you count our votes? Can anybody decide a constitutional amendment with a voice vote? It is not possible,” they argued. Describing the development as a dangerous precedent, the Minority Caucus accused the ruling APC of eroding the rule of law and weakening democratic institutions. “We stand on the side of Nigerians. We stand on the side of the rule of law. We stand on the side of what will make Nigeria a great nation and not what will diminish the integrity, progress and development of this country,” the lawmakers declared. They urged Nigerians to remain vigilant and insisted that while they support the establishment of state police in principle, any constitutional amendment must strictly comply with the provisions of the Constitution and the House Standing Orders. During plenary, no fewer than 311 members of the House of Representatives voted in support of the executive bill seeking to create state police. According to Speaker Abbas Tajudeen, 35 lawmakers voted virtually while the remaining 276 where physically on ground during the voting. This followed the consideration and adoption of the report of the House Committee on the Review of the 1999 Constitution, chaired by Deputy Speaker Benjamin Kalu at plenary. The piece is tilled: “Bill for an Act to Alter the Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria 1999 to provide for the Establishment of State Police Services and for Related Matters (HB. 2797). Vanguard reports that President Bola Tinubu last week transmitted the Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria (Alteration) (State Police) Bill, 2026 to the House of Representatives. The proposed legislation seeks to amend the Constitution of the Federal Republic of Nigeria, 1999, to create a constitutional pathway for the establishment of State Police Services. Tinubu in a letter conveying the bill said the proposal builds on the significant work already done by the House and incorporates additional safeguards to ensure that the creation of a dual policing structure to address the nation’s evolving national security challenges can be achieved quickly and effectively to the benefit of all Nigerians. “This bill builds on the significant work already done in this regard by the House of Representatives and incorporates additional safeguards to ensure that the creation of a dual policing structure to address our nation’s evolving national security challenges can be achieved quickly and effectively to the benefit of all Nigerians. “The proposed legislation is a critical component of our administration’s strategy to reorganize Nigeria’s security architecture to better protect our citizens, and I am confident that the House of Representatives will act quickly to consider and pass this Bill. “While I look forward to expeditious consideration of this submission by the House of Representatives, please accept, Rt. Honourable Speaker, the assurances of my consideration and best regards,” the letter reads. Upon the receipt of the executive bill, the House rescinded its resolution of June 11 2026 on the Establishment of State Police Services and dissolved the 12-member Conference Committee constituted on July 9, 2026. The post Reps minority caucus walks out of plenary over Tinubu’s state police bill appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: El-Rufai’s family disowns purported letter to ADC leadership
items.description: The family of former Kaduna State Governor, Nasir El-Rufai, has dismissed as fake a letter circulating on social media and purportedly addressed to the leadership of the African Democratic Congress. In a statement issued on Thursday by his son, Mohammed Bello El-Rufai, on behalf of the family, the document titled “Urgent Message” and dated July 14, 2026, was described as a fabrication that did not originate from the former governor. According to the statement, the letter, which was purportedly addressed to the National Chairman and National Secretary of the ADC, should be ignored by members of the public. “The former Director-General of the Bureau of Public Enterprises and former Minister of the Federal Capital Territory, Mallam Nasir El-Rufai, CON, wishes to alert the public that a fake letter is in circulation, purported to have been signed by him, titled Urgent Message,” the statement read. It added, “The spurious letter, purportedly dated July 14, 2026, and addressed to the National Chairman and National Secretary of the ADC, should be disregarded.” Press Release: Fake letter written in horrible English and a more horrendous hand writing. ‘The former Director General of the Bureau of Public Enterprises (BPE), former Minister of the Federal Territory (FCT), Mallam Nasir El-Rufai, CON wishes to alert the public that a fake… pic.twitter.com/AjXSRfbCcA — Hon. Mohammed Bello El-Rufai (@B_ELRUFAI) July 23, 2026 The family further maintained that neither the content nor the handwriting in the document belonged to El-Rufai, accusing those behind it of attempting to create confusion. “Neither the poor content nor the handwriting has anything to do with Mallam Nasir El-Rufai,” the statement said. It added that the document represented “another resort to desperation by Mallam’s traducers who seek to sow confusion and division,” describing it as “an instance of the most primitive politics” and urging the public to reject it. The letter circulating online reportedly questioned the emergence of Isah Ashiru as the ADC governorship candidate in Kaduna State. It claimed Ashiru’s nomination had sparked internal tensions because he allegedly failed to complete the party’s online membership registration before the April 30, 2026 deadline, which it described as a constitutional requirement. The purported document also urged the ADC’s national and state leadership to intervene and review the matter. However, El-Rufai’s family insisted that the former governor had no connection with the letter and reiterated its call on the public to disregard it. The post El-Rufai’s family disowns purported letter to ADC leadership appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Gov’t targets GH¢2.3bn from foreign digital platforms through new VAT system
items.description: Finance Minister Dr Cassiel Ato Forson says the government’s planned nationwide rollout of a cross-border technology solution for collecting Value Added Tax (VAT) from non-resident digital platforms is expected to generate about GH¢2.3 billion in its first full year without increasing tax rates. Presenting the 2026 Mid-Year Budget Review in Parliament on Thursday, July 23, Dr Forson said the initiative forms part of the government’s broader strategy to improve tax compliance through technology rather than imposing additional taxes. According to him, the cross-border VAT system was successfully piloted in April 2026, with the trial confirming that the platform is functional, secure and compliant with regulatory requirements. “Government is now moving decisively to secure the necessary regulatory approval for its nationwide rollout. “Upon full deployment, the system is projected to generate about GH¢2.3 billion in its first full year of operation, with revenue expected to grow by about 20% annually,” he said. The Finance Minister stressed that the projected revenue would come without increasing tax rates. “This is sustainable revenue without increasing tax rates. It simply ensures that cross-border digital platforms earning income from Ghanaian customers pay their fair share of taxes,” he stated. Dr Forson said the digital tax initiative complements what he described as the most ambitious reforms to Ghana’s VAT system in more than a decade, aimed at eliminating long-standing distortions, improving efficiency and supporting business growth. He noted that the government abolished the COVID-19 Health Recovery Levy, decoupled the GETFund Levy and the National Health Insurance Levy from the VAT base to make both eligible for input tax deductions, and reduced the effective VAT rate from 21.9% to 20%. The reforms also abolished VAT on reconnaissance and prospecting for minerals, easing the tax burden on thousands of micro and small businesses by removing their obligation to register for and charge VAT. Additionally, the Finance Minister noted that the government has extended the zero-rating of locally manufactured textiles to 2028. Dr Forson said the reforms have simplified the VAT system, removed distortions, lowered the tax burden on businesses, and strengthened the foundation for improved tax compliance. He added that while legislative reforms were necessary, technology would remain central to transforming revenue administration and boosting domestic revenue mobilisation sustainably.

items.title: Adeleke bans NURTW activities in Osun
items.description: …directs police to arrest defiants By Shina Abubakar, Osogbo Osun State Governor Ademola Adeleke has directed that all activities of the National Union of Road Transport Workers, NURTW, across the state should be stopped with immediate effect. This followed the factional crisis that has engulfed the union since Monday, disrupting business activities and heightening insecurity across the state. The governor, in a statement issued by the commissioner for information and public enlightenment, Kolapo Alimi, said the decision was reached after the State Security Council meeting on Thursday. The governor disclosed that collection of levies across all motor parks in the state should stop, among other union activities. He further directed the police and other security agents to arrest anybody that is parading himself as an official/executive of the union in any of our parks. “I hereby direct the police and other security agents to stop all activities of the union in all our parks across the state. I am not saying you should close the parks, but you should not allow anyone to operate in the parks in the name of the union in our parks. “Anyone found contravening this order should be arrested for possible prosecution for disturbance of public peace.” The governor also directed the immediate establishment of the Rapid Response squad, which will consist of all the security agents in the state that will ensure compliance with the directive. The post Adeleke bans NURTW activities in Osun appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Dangote Refinery raises $2.5bn via private equity placement to boost refining
items.description: By Peter Egwuatu Dangote Petroleum Refinery and Petrochemicals FZE (DPRP) has successfully completed a landmark Private Equity Placement that raised approximately US$2.5 billion in new equity, following a highly successful offering. The transaction, which is believed to be Africa’s largest publicly disclosed primary equity private placement, marks a significant milestone in the history of the company and demonstrates strong investor confidence in the refinery’s long-term growth strategy and operational excellence. The capital raise is the first equity funding round involving external investors beyond the company’s legacy shareholder base, underscoring the growing attractiveness of DPRP as a world-class energy and industrial enterprise. According to a statement from Dangote Refinery, the proceeds from the placement will be deployed to support the continued expansion of the refinery and petrochemical complex, strengthen the company’s capital structure, and enhance financial flexibility to pursue future growth opportunities. “The offering attracted broad participation from international and African institutional investors, sovereign-related investment vehicles, development finance institutions, strategic partners, and individual investors. Notable participants included the Africa Finance Corporation (AFC) and India Infra Buildco, an investment vehicle facilitated by the African Export-Import Bank (Afreximbank), reflecting deep and diversified confidence in DPRP’s long-term prospects” the statement noted. Commenting on the successful transaction, Aliko Dangote, President and Chief Executive of Dangote Industries Limited and Chairman of DPRP, described the placement as a strategic milestone in the company’s evolution. “This transaction represents a strategic step to deepen and further institutionalise the Enterprise’s shareholder base, while raising capital to complement our internal cash flows and external funding as DPRP advances its expansion agenda. It also demonstrates our unwavering commitment to developing Africa’s refining and petrochemical capacity, reducing dependence on imported petroleum products and strengthening the continent’s energy security.” Also speaking on the development, David Bird, Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer of Dangote Petroleum Refinery & Petrochemicals, said the overwhelming investor response validates the company’s operational performance and growth outlook. “The exceptional demand we witnessed is a testament to our operational excellence, execution capability and the confidence investors have in DPRP’s leadership and future potential.” With the successful completion of the placement, DPRP is well-positioned to accelerate its long-term growth strategy while strengthening Africa’s energy security through world-scale refining and petrochemical capacity. The strong investor response further reinforces confidence in the company’s vision and its ability to deliver sustainable value over the long term. The company also acknowledged the contributions of its professional advisers and partners whose expertise and support were instrumental in delivering the successful transaction. The post Dangote Refinery raises $2.5bn via private equity placement to boost refining appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: Ato Forson says Gold Board generated $15 billion forex inflows to support Ghana’s reserves
items.description: Finance Minister Dr Cassiel Ato Forson says the Ghana Gold Board generated an additional $15 billion in foreign exchange inflows, describing the intervention as a key factor in strengthening Ghana’s reserves and supporting exchange rate stability. Presenting the 2026 Mid-Year Fiscal Policy Review in Parliament on Thursday, July 23, Dr Forson said the Gold Board was established to formalise the gold trade, reduce smuggling and ensure that a greater share of Ghana’s mineral wealth benefits the country. He said the policy intervention significantly improved Ghana’s external position, contributing to a rise in the country’s current account surplus. “This single policy measure improved Ghana’s current account balance by 6.4 percentage points, from a surplus of 1.9% in 2024 to 8.3% in 2025,” he told Parliament. Dr Forson described the development as “a four-times increment of the current account surplus in just one calendar year.” According to him, the Gold Board initiative was not merely a revenue mobilisation measure but part of government’s broader macroeconomic strategy. “It is a macroeconomic stabilisation policy designed to strengthen the cedi, build external buffers and restore confidence in the Ghanaian economy,” he said. The Finance Minister said government is seeking to consolidate the gains through the Ghana Accelerated National Reserve Accumulation Policy (GANRAP), which aims to increase international reserves to the equivalent of 15 months of import cover by the end of 2028. He added that the government has reached an agreement with large-scale mining companies for the purchase of 30% of their annual gold production for local refining. According to Dr Forson, the arrangement will support domestic value addition while helping Ghana accumulate reserves. He also disclosed that government has amended the Bank of Ghana Act to make inflation targeting a shared responsibility between the Ministry of Finance and the central bank. The move, he said, is intended to strengthen coordination between fiscal and monetary policies. The Ghana Gold Board has been one of the major economic interventions introduced by the Mahama administration, with government positioning it as a mechanism to formalise the small-scale gold sector and retain more value from Ghana’s gold exports.

items.title: What are the main bases housing US forces in the Middle East?
items.description: During its war with the United States, Iran has targeted Middle East bases where American troops are stationed, turning assets once seen as a bulwark against Tehran into targets. Below, AFP examines some of the major concentrations of US forces in the region, which fall under the US military’s Central Command (Centcom). Elizabeth Dent, a senior fellow at the Washington Institute for Near East Policy, said Middle East bases housing US troops “can support a wide range of missions from logistics, pre-positioning assets, refuelling, and of course combat operations”. Iran, in justifying its own retaliatory strikes, has accused Gulf states of allowing the US to use the bases to launch attacks, which Dent said was likely, though Tehran’s “perennial argument that all of these bases are inherently offensive is not accurate”. Bahrain The small kingdom hosts the US Navy’s Fifth Fleet and US Naval Forces Central Command headquarters. Bahrain’s deep-water port can accommodate the largest US military vessels, such as aircraft carriers, and the US Navy has used the base since 1948, when it was operated by Britain’s Royal Navy. US ships including anti-mine vessels and logistical support ships have their home port in Bahrain. Bahrain has faced heavy Iranian strikes, and early in the war, Manama briefly evacuated the neighborhood hosting the Fifth Fleet as it came under attack. Iraq Iraq is a close Iran ally, but also a strategic US partner. American troops are deployed as part of the US-led coalition to fight the Islamic State group in Iraq’s northern Kurdistan region, mostly at the Erbil airport and al-Harir air base. The coalition is preparing to end its mission in Iraq by the end of September. Iraqi groups have hit US facilities, including diplomatic missions in Baghdad and Erbil, more than 600 times during the war. Last week, a US service member was killed in northern Iraq during the controlled detonation of unexploded ordnance from a downed Iranian drone. Jordan While Iran often refers to “American bases” in Jordan, Amman and Washington maintain that the sites are Jordanian bases housing US troops. In the run-up to the February 28 strikes on Iran that launched the war, the US reportedly deployed dozens of military aircraft to Jordan’s Muwaffaq Salti Air Base, which also houses other Western forces. The base has F-16, F-15 and F-35 fighter jets, as well as refuelling and reconnaissance aircraft, drones, and Patriot and THAAD air defense systems. Iranian attacks recently killed three US service members in Jordan. “In recent years, largely because of Iran’s growing missile, drone, and proxy network, the US shifted toward more nimble basing along the western side of the Middle East region” in Israel and Jordan, according to Dent, a former director for the Gulf and Arabian Peninsula in the Office of the US Secretary of Defence. Kuwait The US has had a significant military presence in Kuwait after coming to its aid when Iraq invaded in 1990. It has several US bases , including Camp Arifjan, housing the forward headquarters for the US Army component of Centcom. Ali al-Salem Air Base hosts the 386th Air Expeditionary Wing, which serves as a primary airlift hub and gateway to deliver combat power to joint and coalition forces in the region. Washington has also deployed drones including MQ-9 Reapers to Kuwait. Six US service members have been killed there since the war began, as well as four Kuwaiti troops and border guards. Qatar Qatar houses the Al Udeid Air Base , which includes forward components of Centcom, as well the command’s air forces and special operations forces. The largest US base in the Middle East, it hosts rotating combat aircraft and the 379th Air Expeditionary Wing, which includes airlift, aerial refuelling, intelligence, surveillance, reconnaissance and medical evacuation assets. Iran hit Al Udeid during the current war, having first attacked it in June 2025 following US strikes targeting Iranian nuclear facilities. United Arab Emirates The UAE’s Al Dhafra Air Base hosts the US 380th Air Expeditionary Wing, a force composed of 10 squadrons of aircraft that also includes drones such as MQ-9 Reapers. Combat aircraft have rotated through Al Dhafra, which also hosts the US Air Force’s hub for advanced air warfare training. Saudi Arabia US forces returned to Saudi Arabia in 2019 following a bilateral agreement. The kingdom does not have American bases but it hosts troops at its Prince Sultan Air Base in Al-Kharj, outside Riyadh, which houses the 378th Air Expeditionary Wing and has come under attack. A US service member in Saudi Arabia was killed after sustaining wounds in an Iranian attack in March. Despite deadly attacks, Chatham House Middle East expert Neil Quilliam said he did not expect Gulf states to contemplate reducing the US military presence, “though DC itself, is likely to reconsider its military posture in the region”.

items.title: Biohacking: How sunlight can prolong your life, according to Dr Naa Asheley Ashietey
items.description: Early morning sunlight could be one of the simplest and most effective ways to improve health, slow biological ageing and enhance longevity, according to Dr Naa Asheley Ashietey, founder of Nova Wellness Center. Speaking on Joy FM’s Ultimate Health programme with Nortey Dua on Sunday, July 19, Dr Ashietey urged Ghanaians to embrace what she described as “basic biohacks” that are readily available and cost nothing, beginning with regular exposure to natural sunlight. She explained that while biohacking is often associated with expensive technology and advanced treatments, some of the most effective interventions are simple lifestyle practices that almost anyone can adopt. “I’ve come up with a list of 10 biohacks that are at our fingertips. They don’t cost any money. They are very basic, and if you’re able to master these, it’s going to go a long way to help. If not, then we can look into the advanced modalities.” Dr Ashietey identified sunlight as the number one biohack, saying many people in Ghana fail to take advantage of the country’s abundant sunshine despite living in a tropical climate. She observed that modern lifestyles often keep people indoors throughout the day. “We wake up, get into our cars, drive to work, stay inside the office all day, and then drive back home. We simply don’t get enough sunlight,” she said. According to her, spending between 15 and 20 minutes outdoors in the early morning—preferably between 7:00 a.m. and 10:00 a.m.—can significantly improve vitamin D production. She explained that vitamin D plays a crucial role in hormone production and overall health. “Vitamin D is extremely important because it is a precursor for many of our hormones. One of the most important is melatonin, the hormone that helps us sleep.” Dr Ashietey noted that exposure to early morning sunlight helps regulate the body’s circadian rhythm—the internal biological clock responsible for controlling sleep and wakefulness. According to her, morning sunlight signals to the body that it is time to become active, while sunset prepares it for rest. “When you wake up and go into the sun, you’re telling your body it’s time to begin the day. Later, when the sun goes down, your body naturally prepares for sleep.” She argued that this natural process has become disrupted as more people spend long hours indoors or remain glued to electronic devices. READ ALSO: Nova Wellness CEO urges Ghanaians to embrace simple ‘biohacks’ for better health and longer lives The result, she said, is a growing number of people suffering from insomnia and poor-quality sleep. Dr Ashietey stressed that quality sleep is one of the most important components of healthy ageing. She recommended that adults with a regular working schedule should aim to be in bed between 9:00 p.m. and 10:00 p.m. and obtain at least six to seven hours of uninterrupted sleep each night. “It’s only when you sleep that your brain removes toxins, rejuvenates and gets you ready for the next day.” She explained that the brain’s glymphatic system becomes active during deep sleep, clearing waste products that accumulate throughout the day. According to her, poor sleep has been associated with memory loss, anxiety, depression and cognitive decline. Dr Ashietey also referred to research suggesting that many Alzheimer’s disease patients had a history of chronic insomnia before developing the condition, highlighting what she described as a significant relationship between insufficient sleep and long-term brain health. The wellness expert cautioned against taking mobile phones, televisions and other electronic devices to bed. She said the light emitted by screens keeps the brain stimulated and suppresses the natural production of melatonin, making it harder to fall asleep. “Most of us go to bed with our phones, and we don’t realise that the light from these devices keeps the brain active even when we’re trying to sleep.” She encouraged people to create a sleep-friendly environment by keeping bedrooms dark, quiet and free from unnecessary distractions. Recognising that healthcare professionals and other shift workers often have irregular sleep schedules, Dr Ashietey said they should still try to obtain morning sunlight whenever possible. She recommended that those returning home after night shifts spend a short period outdoors before sleeping to support vitamin D production. She also advised shift workers to create an artificial night-time environment during daytime sleep by blocking out light, reducing noise and avoiding electronic devices. “Our bodies are intelligent and can adapt to different schedules, but we still need to support them by creating the right conditions for quality rest.” Dr Ashietey also discussed advances in wellness assessments that measure biological rather than chronological age. She said tools that analyse heart rate variability can assess how the nervous system responds to stress and estimate whether an individual’s body is ageing faster or slower than expected. Such assessments, she explained, provide insight into overall health and resilience, helping individuals make informed lifestyle changes before chronic illnesses develop. Turning to nutrition, Dr Ashietey said intermittent fasting can improve metabolic health and support weight management when practised correctly. However, she warned that extended fasts lasting 24 to 72 hours are not suitable for everyone. According to her, people should first understand their overall health status before attempting prolonged fasting, as excessive fasting can place additional stress on the body. “If you finish dinner around six in the evening and have breakfast at seven or eight the next morning without snacking, you’ve already completed about 14 hours of intermittent fasting.” She said this approach allows much of the fasting period to occur during sleep, making it easier on the body while still offering potential metabolic benefits. Throughout the discussion, Dr Ashietey maintained that the foundations of good health are often simple rather than expensive. She encouraged Ghanaians to take advantage of natural resources such as sunlight, establish consistent sleep routines, minimise screen exposure before bedtime and adopt healthier eating habits. According to her, these basic biohacks can improve energy levels, enhance cognitive function, reduce stress and potentially contribute to healthier ageing and longer life.

items.title: Fraude aux examens à Kindia : le parquet fait appel, les élèves transférés à Conakry
items.description: Le parquet près le Tribunal de première instance de Kindia a décidé d’interjeter appel du jugement rendu dans l’affaire des élèves condamnés pour fraude lors des examens nationaux. Selon les informations recueillies, les élèves concernés sont actuellement en cours de transfert vers Conakry, où ils devront comparaître devant la Cour d’appel pour un nouvel examen du dossier en second ressort. Cette nouvelle étape judiciaire intervient quelques jours après le décès de l’un des prévenus, Mamadou Djouma Bah. Le candidat est décédé en début de semaine à l’Hôpital régional de Kindia, où il avait été admis à la suite d’une maladie. Lors des réquisitions devant le tribunal, le procureur avait requis une peine assortie de sursis. À Kindia, l’émotion demeure vive. Depuis l’annonce du décès de Mamadou Djouma Bah, plusieurs de ses camarades poursuivent leurs manifestations pour dénoncer la situation et réclamer une révision des décisions prises dans cette affaire. Aboubacar Dramé, correspondant régional à Kindia +224 623 08 09 10 L’article Fraude aux examens à Kindia : le parquet fait appel, les élèves transférés à Conakry est apparu en premier sur Mediaguinee.com .

items.title: 5th anniversary of Olu of Warri: Itsekiri Global Homecoming partners Overland Airways
items.description: ..As Capt Boyo hails monarch By Matilda Ikediobi As activities marking the fifth coronation anniversary of the Olu of Warri, Ogiame Atuwatse III, gather momentum, the organisers of the Itsekiri Global Homecoming, IGHC, have announced Overland Airways as the official airline partner for the week long celebration. The Homecoming, scheduled to hold in Warri, Delta State, from August 16 to August 22, 2026, is one of the major events lined up to commemorate the monarch’s fifth year on the throne. It is expected to attract hundreds of Itsekiri sons and daughters, as well as friends of the Iwere Nation, from across Nigeria and the diaspora. Under the partnership, Overland Airways will provide discounted airfares for participants travelling into Osubi Airport through its Lagos and Abuja routes, a move aimed at making travel easier for attendees. A member of the Royal Order of Iwere, RoI, and a leader of the Itsekiri Global Homecoming initiative, Mr. Ade Mabo, described the partnership as strategic, saying reliable air connectivity was essential to the success of the celebration. “We are delighted to welcome Overland Airways as our official airline partner for this historic Homecoming. A gathering of this magnitude requires robust and excellent logistics to bring our diaspora family back to their ancestral home smoothly and safely. “Overland Airways’ long standing commitment to Delta State and their direct service to Osubi Airport make them the natural partner for this landmark event. Through this collaboration, we are not only easing travel for our attendees but also setting a precedent for strategic partnerships that drive socio economic growth in the Warri Kingdom,” he said. The Chief Executive Officer of Overland Airways, Capt. Edward Boyo, congratulated Ogiame Atuwatse III on the fifth anniversary of his ascension to the throne, saying the airline was proud to support the Homecoming. According to him, “The management and staff of Overland Airways congratulate His Majesty, Ogiame Atuwatse III, on the fifth anniversary of ascending the throne of his fathers and join millions of Itsekiris worldwide to pray for health, peace and prosperity during his reign. “Overland Airways is proud to be associated with the Itsekiri Global Homecoming. This celebration aligns perfectly with our 24 year commitment to providing safe air connectivity and promoting economic, cultural and trade relations within the communities we serve.” The airline resumed operations to Osubi Airport in 2025 with its Embraer E175 aircraft.. The Itsekiri Global Homecoming is expected to showcase the cultural heritage of the Iwere Nation, deepen ties among Itsekiris at home and abroad, promote investment opportunities and serve as one of the flagship events commemorating the fifth coronation anniversary of the Olu of Warri. The post 5th anniversary of Olu of Warri: Itsekiri Global Homecoming partners Overland Airways appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: NCAA threatens to suspend Royal Air Maroc operations over passenger complaints
items.description: By Dickson Omobola Nigeria Civil Aviation Authority, NCAA, Wednesday, threatened to stop Royal Air Maroc’s operations in Nigeria over what it described as incessant disregard of Nigerian passengers and violations of consumer protection regulations. Director of Public Affairs and Consumer Protection at the NCAA, Mr Michael Achimugu, accused the Moroccan carrier of poor passenger handling, frequent baggage infractions and failing to promptly resolve complaints lodged by Nigerian travellers. Achimugu, in a statement on X, said despite being sanctioned in 2025 for consumer protection-related infractions, the airline had shown little improvement in its treatment of Nigerian passengers. His words: “The airline, Royal Air Maroc, has for the longest time, been the biggest culprit when it comes to baggage infractions, poor passenger handling, and extremely poor resolution processes when dealing with Nigerian passengers. “Having been sanctioned – for the first time ever – for consumer protection-related infractions in 2025, one would have expected very major improvements in the way they attend to complaints from Nigerian passengers. “Sadly, despite the best possible support from the authority (we support all airlines to continue to be in business), the manner of response to these issues have hardly seen much by way of improvement; baggage continue to be shortlanded without timely information to affected passengers, first need is not being paid on a consistent level, and complaints are treated mostly after the NCAA gets involved and much pressure is given. “To make matters worse, invitations from the CAA are treated with a degree of insolence that is unacceptable. Everywhere in the world, when the CAA summons an airline, it is mandatory to attend. “Royal Air Maroc’s country manager, Ahmed Boussouf, will sit in Lagos and refuse to attend meetings in Abuja. Instead, the airline will hastily send an email on the eve of the meeting that the cases are being processed, and thereafter, send one Theophilus (their Abuja rep), without authority to resolve cases or make commitments. “Today, one of those events occurred. In registering our displeasure over this incessant disregard for Nigerian passengers and the Authority, this country manager, Boussouf, said to me, ‘whatever you want to do, do’. “While our job may be thankless and exhausting, the NCAA, under the dispensation of President Bola Tinubu; Minister Festus Keyamo; and the Director General of the NCAA, Capt. Chris Najomo, will never be diminished to such a level of brazen disregard and non-compliance. “The passengers whose cases have been delayed, quite unfairly and unnecessarily, are paying, loyal customers. International airlines have bilateral service agreements (BASA) to honour, as well as the obligation of simple service to passengers. “Strict enforcement will happen against this airline and my department will push for a stoppage of Royal Air Maroc operations in Nigeria until they make a commitment to treat Nigerian passengers better. “I am not the kind of public servant who is afraid to lose his job for the right reasons. I will never accept this behaviour from operators. It is not arrogance; it is simply adherence to rules that have, for ages, been treated like they don’t exist. “Whatever gains we make in this industry, we will continue to fail if the rights and responsibilities of all stakeholders are treated as an afterthought. We must be intentional and systematic, adhering to world class standards. “Most airlines are fortunate, and they know it, that Nigerian passengers do not have a culture of boycotting airlines, no matter the shortcomings. Otherwise, some of these cases would not even require regulatory intervention if any operator fails to take their customers seriously. There is nowhere RAM flies to, that people do not have alternatives in Air Algerie, Egypt Air, and Ethiopian. “My department will be taking a very firm stand over this matter, and Royal Air Maroc will stop taking for granted the relationship Morocco has enjoyed with Nigeria and Nigerians. We will ensure stiffer penalties against Royal Air Maroc this time. The job we were given to do will be done without fear.” The post NCAA threatens to suspend Royal Air Maroc operations over passenger complaints appeared first on Vanguard News .

items.title: KADIOGO/KOUBRI : Une grande marche patriotique de soutien au Capitaine Ibrahim Traoré mobilise les forces vives
items.description: KADIOGO/KOUBRI : Une grande marche patriotique de soutien au Capitaine Ibrahim Traoré mobilise les forces vives Koubri, 18 juil. 2026 (AIB)-Les forces vives de la commune de Koubri ont massivement pris part, samedi, à une grande marche patriotique de soutien au Président du Faso, le Capitaine Ibrahim Traoré. Placée sous le signe de l'unité, de la cohésion sociale, du vivre-ensemble et de la solidarité nationale, la marche est partie du commissariat de police de Koubri pour s'achever à la nouvelle mairie de la commune. Répondant à l'appel du camarade Abdoul Moubarak Nikièma, de la Brigade des Volontaires pour la Défense de la Patrie (VDP) version Agriculture, les autorités administratives, paramilitaires, coutumières et religieuses, les serviteurs du peuple, la coordination communale des associations de veille citoyenne, la coordination communale des femmes, le Conseil communal des personnes handicapées, les Comités villageois de développement (CVD), les associations et mouvements de jeunesse ainsi que la coordination communale des commerçants ont pris une part active à cette manifestation. Le chargé de mission de la Présidence du Faso pour le Kadiogo, le camarade Patrick Gampiné, le premier vice-président de la commune de Ouagadougou, le camarade Dieudonné Ouédraogo, ainsi que le représentant du président du mois de la Coordination nationale des associations de veille citoyenne (CNAVC), le camarade Alassane Ouédraogo, ont également honoré la cérémonie de leur présence. Dans une ambiance chaleureuse, rythmée par des chants patriotiques, des slogans révolutionnaires et des messages d'encouragement à l'endroit des autorités, des Forces de défense et de sécurité (FDS) et des Volontaires pour la défense de la patrie (VDP), les marcheurs ont parcouru près de trois kilomètres, réaffirmant leur adhésion aux idéaux du Capitaine Ibrahim Traoré et de la Révolution progressiste populaire (RPP). À l'arrivée, une cérémonie ponctuée de plusieurs interventions a été organisée. S'exprimant au nom du chef de canton de Koubri, le Songkolg-Naaba a transmis les salutations de Sa Majesté le Naaba Kangré, avant de saluer l'initiative et d'inviter les populations à demeurer unies et engagées aux côtés des autorités de la Transition. Au cours de la cérémonie, le camarade Gampiné a procédé, au nom du Chef de l'État, à la remise d'un étendard au Président de la Délégation spéciale (PDS) de Koubri, symbole d'unité nationale, de patriotisme et de souveraineté. Prenant la parole, l'initiateur de la marche, le camarade Nikièma, a félicité les populations pour leur mobilisation exemplaire et s'est réjoui du bon déroulement de l'événement. « Ce rassemblement est avant tout un appel à l'unité, à la solidarité, à la cohésion sociale et au vivre-ensemble. Nous exprimons notre soutien indéfectible au Capitaine Ibrahim Traoré et aux institutions de notre pays dans leurs efforts pour renforcer la sécurité, préserver la souveraineté nationale et promouvoir le développement du Burkina Faso. Nous rendons également un vibrant hommage aux FDS et aux VDP qui consentent d'immenses sacrifices pour la préservation de l'intégrité territoriale et de la stabilité du pays », a-t-il déclaré. Le représentant du président du mois de la CNAVC a, lui aussi, rendu un hommage appuyé au Président du Faso, aux FDS et aux VDP pour les sacrifices consentis dans la lutte pour la reconquête du territoire national. Selon lui, la forte mobilisation observée à Koubri constitue un signal fort de l'engagement des populations en faveur d'une souveraineté pleinement assumée. Pour sa part, le Président de la Délégation spéciale de Koubri, Samadé Léonard Gougou, a salué la mobilisation exceptionnelle des forces vives ainsi que la présence des autorités venues soutenir l'initiative. Il a indiqué que cette marche traduisait l'adhésion des populations de Koubri aux idéaux portés par le Capitaine Ibrahim Traoré. « Koubri se tient debout ce matin, dans l'unité et la solidarité, pour hisser très haut l'étendard du Burkina Faso. C'est la preuve que nous demeurons engagés aux côtés de notre Président dans son combat pour une souveraineté retrouvée. Les forces vives de notre commune sont déterminées à accompagner les autorités de la Révolution progressiste populaire dans l'édification d'un Burkina Faso libre, digne et prospère », a-t-il affirmé. Avant de clore la cérémonie, le PDS a exprimé sa reconnaissance aux populations, à la marraine de l'événement, aux Forces de défense et de sécurité, à la Police municipale, aux autorités coutumières et religieuses, au chargé de mission de la Présidence du Faso ainsi qu'aux professionnels des médias pour leur contribution au succès de cette manifestation patriotique. Agence d'information du Burkina LZ/C-AIB Koubri
